#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;

use smoltcp::iface::{Config, Interface, SocketSet, QueryInterface};
use smoltcp::phy::Checksum;
use smoltcp::socket::{TcpSocket, UdpSocket, Socket};
use smoltcp::wire::{EthernetAddress, HardwareAddress, IpAddress, IpCidr, Ipv4Address, ETHERNET_MTU};
use smoltcp::time::Instant;

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall2, syscall3, SYS_LOG, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, SYS_TIME};

mod aethernet_device;
use aethernet_device::AetherNetDevice;

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// Get current time from kernel
fn get_current_time_ms() -> u64 {
    // Assuming SYS_TIME returns ticks, convert to ms for smoltcp Instant
    unsafe { syscall2(SYS_TIME, 0, 0) * 10 } // Assuming 1 tick = 10 ms for demo
}


// --- IPC API for other V-Nodes (Socket API) ---
// (Using postcard serialization for real IPC)
#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
enum NetStackRequest {
    OpenSocket(u32, u16), // type (0=TCP, 1=UDP), local_port (0 for ephemeral)
    Send(u32, Vec<u8>), // socket_handle, data
    Recv(u32), // socket_handle
    CloseSocket(u32), // socket_handle
}

#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
enum NetStackResponse {
    SocketOpened(u32), // socket_handle
    Data(Vec<u8>),
    Error(u32), // error_code
    Success,
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For simplicity, hardcode channel ID for aethernet-service
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(3); // Channel to receive requests from other V-Nodes
    let net_bridge_channel_id = 2; // Channel to communicate with net-bridge

    log("AetherNet Service V-Node starting up...");

    // Allocate a DMA buffer for the AetherNetDevice to use for RX (it's managed by the kernel)
    let rx_dma_handle = match aethernet_device::net_alloc_buf(ETHERNET_MTU as usize) {
        Ok(h) => {
            log("AetherNet: Allocated RX DMA buffer for device.");
            h
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Failed to allocate RX DMA buffer for device: {}", e));
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    // 1. Initialize AetherNetDevice to interact with the net-bridge driver
    // Pass the net_bridge_channel_id to AetherNetDevice::new
    let mut device = AetherNetDevice::new(0, net_bridge_channel_id);

    // 2. Configure smoltcp interface
    let ethernet_addr = EthernetAddress([0x02, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01]);
    let config = Config::new(HardwareAddress::Ethernet(ethernet_addr));
    let mut iface = Interface::new(config, &mut device, Instant::from_millis(get_current_time_ms()));

    // Assign a static IP address
    iface.update_ip_addrs(|addrs| {
        addrs.push(IpCidr::new(IpAddress::v4(10, 0, 2, 15), 24)).unwrap();
    });
    log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: IP Address set to {}", IpAddress::v4(10,0,2,15)));

    // 3. Initialize smoltcp SocketSet
    let mut sockets_storage = Vec::new();
    let mut sockets = SocketSet::new(sockets_storage);

    // 4. Socket Management
    let mut next_socket_handle: u32 = 1;
    let mut socket_handles: BTreeMap<u32, smoltcp::socket::SocketHandle> = BTreeMap::new();

    // Main event loop for the network stack
    loop {
        let timestamp = Instant::from_millis(get_current_time_ms());

        // 1. Poll smoltcp interface for network events (e.g., ARP, ICMP, TCP/UDP activity)
        // This call will trigger device.receive() and device.transmit() internally
        iface.poll(timestamp, &mut device, &mut sockets);

        // 2. Process incoming requests from other V-Nodes (Socket API)
        // Use a non-blocking receive to ensure smoltcp polling is not starved
        if let Ok(Some(req_data)) = own_chan.recv_non_blocking() {
            if let Ok(request) = postcard::from_bytes::<NetStackRequest>(&req_data) {
                log("AetherNet: Received request from another V-Node.");
                let response = match request {
                    NetStackRequest::OpenSocket(sock_type, local_port) => {
                        let handle = next_socket_handle;
                        next_socket_handle += 1;

                        let socket = match sock_type {
                            0 => { // TCP
                                log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Opening TCP socket on port {}", local_port));
                                let mut socket = TcpSocket::new(
                                    smoltcp::socket::TcpSocketBuffer::new(Vec::new()),
                                    smoltcp::socket::TcpSocketBuffer::new(Vec::new()),
                                );
                                if local_port != 0 { socket.listen(local_port).unwrap(); }
                                socket
                            },
                            1 => { // UDP
                                log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Opening UDP socket on port {}", local_port));
                                let mut socket = UdpSocket::new(
                                    smoltcp::socket::UdpSocketBuffer::new(Vec::new()),
                                    smoltcp::socket::UdpSocketBuffer::new(Vec::new()),
                                );
                                if local_port != 0 { socket.bind(local_port).unwrap(); }
                                socket
                            },
                            _ => {
                                log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Invalid socket type {}", sock_type));
                                NetStackResponse::Error(100) // Invalid socket type
                            }
                        };

                        // Add socket to management
                        sockets.add(socket);
                        socket_handles.insert(handle, smoltcp::socket::SocketHandle::from(sockets.len() - 1)); // Correctly get smoltcp handle
                        NetStackResponse::SocketOpened(handle)
                    },
                    NetStackRequest::Send(handle, data) => {
                        log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Sending {} bytes on socket {}", data.len(), handle));
                        if let Some(smoltcp_handle) = socket_handles.get(&handle) {
                            if let Some(socket) = sockets.get_mut(*smoltcp_handle) {
                                match socket {
                                    smoltcp::socket::Socket::Tcp(s) => {
                                        // For simplicity, assume all data can be sent immediately
                                        s.send_slice(&data).unwrap_or(0);
                                        NetStackResponse::Success
                                    },
                                    smoltcp::socket::Socket::Udp(s) => {
                                        // UDP send needs destination. This API is simplified.
                                        NetStackResponse::Error(101) // UDP send requires dest IP/Port
                                    },
                                    _ => NetStackResponse::Error(102), // Not a TCP/UDP socket
                                }
                            } else { NetStackResponse::Error(103) } // Smoltcp Socket not found
                        } else { NetStackResponse::Error(103) } // Our handle not found
                    },
                    NetStackRequest::Recv(handle) => {
                        log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Receiving on socket {}", handle));
                        if let Some(smoltcp_handle) = socket_handles.get(&handle) {
                             if let Some(socket) = sockets.get_mut(*smoltcp_handle) {
                                match socket {
                                    smoltcp::socket::Socket::Tcp(s) => {
                                        let mut buffer = Vec::new(); // Or use a pre-allocated buffer
                                        if let Ok(size) = s.recv_slice(&mut buffer) {
                                            buffer.resize(size, 0);
                                            NetStackResponse::Data(buffer)
                                        }
                                         else {
                                            NetStackResponse::Data(Vec::new()) // No data
                                        }
                                    },
                                    smoltcp::socket::Socket::Udp(s) => {
                                        let mut buffer = Vec::new();
                                        if let Ok((size, _endpoint)) = s.recv_slice(&mut buffer) {
                                            buffer.resize(size, 0);
                                            NetStackResponse::Data(buffer)
                                        }
                                         else {
                                            NetStackResponse::Data(Vec::new())
                                        }
                                    },
                                    _ => NetStackResponse::Error(102), // Not a TCP/UDP socket
                                }
                            } else { NetStackResponse::Error(103) } // Smoltcp Socket not found
                        } else { NetStackResponse::Error(103) } // Our handle not found
                    },
                    NetStackRequest::CloseSocket(handle) => {
                        log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Closing socket {}", handle));
                        if let Some(smoltcp_handle) = socket_handles.remove(&handle) {
                            sockets.remove(*smoltcp_handle); // Remove from smoltcp SocketSet
                            NetStackResponse::Success
                        }
                        else {
                            NetStackResponse::Error(103) // Socket not found
                        }
                    },
                };
                own_chan.send(&response).unwrap_or_else(|_| log("AetherNet: Failed to send response."));
            } else {
                log("AetherNet: Failed to deserialize NetStackRequest.");
            }
        }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
pub extern "C" fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("AetherNet Service V-Node panicked!");
    loop {}
}

print(rust_code)

print("Creating src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs to implement SwarmTransport.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmTransport, SwarmError};
use crate::arp_dht::PeerInfo;
use libnexus_net::{NetClient, NetError};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

pub struct NexusNetTransport {
    net_client: NetClient,
    udp_socket_handle: u32, // Re-use a single UDP socket for all fetches
}

impl NexusNetTransport {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, NetError> {
        let mut net_client = NetClient::new();
        let udp_socket_handle = net_client.open_udp_socket(0)?; // Open an ephemeral UDP socket
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Opened UDP socket with handle: {}", udp_socket_handle));
        Ok(NexusNetTransport {
            net_client,
            udp_socket_handle,
        })
    }
}

impl SwarmTransport for NexusNetTransport {
    fn fetch_chunk(&self, peer: &PeerInfo, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Fetching chunk {} from peer {}:{}",
            alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes()), peer.ip_address[0], peer.port));

        // Serialize CID for sending
        let request_payload = postcard::to_allocvec(&cid).map_err(|_| SwarmError::NetworkError)?;

        // Send CID request to the peer over UDP
        self.net_client.send_to(
            self.udp_socket_handle,
            peer.ip_address,
            peer.port,
            request_payload
        ).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to send request: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // Receive the response (chunk data)
        // This will block until a response is received or a timeout occurs
        // In a real system, we'd have a more robust async receive with timeouts
        let response_payload = self.net_client.recv(self.udp_socket_handle).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to receive response: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // In a real scenario, the response payload would be verified and parsed to extract the chunk data.
        // For this sketch, we assume the response_payload IS the chunk data.
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Received {} bytes for chunk {}", response_payload.len(), alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes())));
        Ok(response_payload)
    }
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

// Import NetClient and NetError from libnexus-net

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes (redefined for registry to avoid module conflict)
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error */ }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // Net-bridge's own channel ID (to receive IRQ events from kernel)
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(1); // Channel for RegistryService's IPC

    log("Registry V-Node starting up...");

    // 1. Initialize NexusNetTransport (which uses NetClient internally)
    let transport = match NexusNetTransport::new() {
        Ok(t) => {
            log("Registry: NexusNetTransport initialized.");
            t
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to initialize NexusNetTransport: {:?}", e));
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    // --- Swarm Engine Initialization ---
    let trust = TrustStore::new();
    let mut dht = InMemoryDht { values: BTreeMap::new(), peers: Vec::new(), id: NodeId([0; 32]), trust: TrustStore::new() }; // Initialize DHT with a dummy NodeId and TrustStore

    // Add some dummy peers for DHT to find
    dht.add_peer(PeerInfo {
        id: NodeId([0xAA; 32]),
        aid: crate::trust::Aid([0xBB; 32]),
        ip_address: [10, 0, 2, 1], // Example peer IP
        port: 60000, // Example peer port
    });

    // Load a dummy package for demonstration
    let (manifest, chunks) = crate::examples::hello_package::make_hello_package();
    dht.store(manifest.root_cid, crate::arp_dht::DhtValue::Manifest(manifest.clone()));

    let mut swarm = SwarmEngine::new(transport, dht);
    // --- End Swarm Engine Initialization ---

    // Simulate fetching a package from the swarm using the real network transport
    match swarm.fetch_package(&manifest) {
        Ok(data) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Successfully fetched package '{}' ({} bytes)", manifest.name, data.len()));
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to fetch package: {:?}", e));
        }
    }

    loop {
        // Original RegistryService loop logic would go here, handling IPC requests
        // For now, we simulate just logging to avoid complexity
        log("Registry V-Node idling...");
        // In a real V-Node, it would be waiting for IPC messages
        let _ = own_chan.recv_blocking(); // Block and wait for IPC messages
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Registry V-Node panicked!");
    loop {} // Keep looping in case of panic
}
'''

// src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmTransport, SwarmError};
use crate::arp_dht::PeerInfo;
use libnexus_net::{NetClient, NetError};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

pub struct NexusNetTransport {
    net_client: NetClient,
    udp_socket_handle: u32, // Re-use a single UDP socket for all fetches
}

impl NexusNetTransport {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, NetError> {
        let mut net_client = NetClient::new();
        let udp_socket_handle = net_client.open_udp_socket(0)?; // Open an ephemeral UDP socket
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Opened UDP socket with handle: {}", udp_socket_handle));
        Ok(NexusNetTransport {
            net_client,
            udp_socket_handle,
        })
    }
}

impl SwarmTransport for NexusNetTransport {
    fn fetch_chunk(&self, peer: &PeerInfo, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Fetching chunk {} from peer {}:{}",
            alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes()), peer.ip_address[0], peer.port));

        // Serialize CID for sending
        let request_payload = postcard::to_allocvec(&cid).map_err(|_| SwarmError::NetworkError)?;

        // Send CID request to the peer over UDP
        self.net_client.send_to(
            self.udp_socket_handle,
            peer.ip_address,
            peer.port,
            request_payload
        ).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to send request: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // Receive the response (chunk data)
        // This will block until a response is received or a timeout occurs
        // In a real system, we'd have a more robust async receive with timeouts
        let response_payload = self.net_client.recv(self.udp_socket_handle).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to receive response: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // In a real scenario, the response payload would be verified and parsed to extract the chunk data.
        // For this sketch, we assume the response_payload IS the chunk data.
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Received {} bytes for chunk {}", response_payload.len(), alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes())));
        Ok(response_payload)
    }
}
'''

#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::registry_service::RegistryService;

// Import NexusNetTransport
use crate::swarm_engine::nexus_net_transport::NexusNetTransport;
use crate::swarm_engine::global_search::GlobalSearchService; // Import GlobalSearchService

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes (redefined for registry to avoid module conflict)
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error */ }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // Net-bridge's own channel ID (to receive IRQ events from kernel)
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(1); // Channel for RegistryService's IPC

    log("Registry V-Node starting up...");

    // 1. Initialize NexusNetTransport (which uses NetClient internally)
    let transport = match NexusNetTransport::new() {
        Ok(t) => {
            log("Registry: NexusNetTransport initialized.");
            t
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to initialize NexusNetTransport: {:?}", e));
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    // --- Swarm Engine Initialization ---
    let trust_store = TrustStore::new();
    let local_aid = Aid([0xCD; 32]); // Dummy local AID
    let local_node_id = NodeId([0; 32]); // Dummy NodeId for local DHT

"""## Implement DHT Keyspace & NodeID Routing

### Subtask:
Implement the DHT Keyspace and NodeID routing for the Aether Registry Protocol (ARP) in the `registry-service` V-Node.

**Reasoning**:
The first instruction is to update the `InMemoryDht` struct and its `Dht` trait implementation in `src/arp_dht.rs` to incorporate Kademlia-like XOR metric for NodeID distances and routing table management. This involves defining a `NodeId` and implementing a `distance` method using XOR. The `find_peers` method will then sort peers by this distance. I will ensure `PeerInfo` is correctly aligned with `NodeId` and `Aid` for a cleaner design.
"""

print("Updating src/arp_dht.rs for DHT Keyspace and NodeID routing.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/arp_dht.rs
#![allow(dead_code)]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::trust::Aid; // Assuming Aid is defined in trust.rs

// A 256-bit Node ID for Kademlia-like DHT
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]
pub struct NodeId(pub [u8; 32]);

impl NodeId {
    /// Calculates the XOR distance between two NodeIds.
    pub fn distance(&self, other: &NodeId) -> [u8; 32] {
        let mut dist = [0u8; 32];
        for i in 0..32 {
            dist[i] = self.0[i] ^ other.0[i];
        }
        dist
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct PeerInfo {
    pub id: NodeId,     // The NodeId of the peer
    pub aid: Aid,       // The Aether Identity of the peer
    pub ip_address: [u8; 4], // IP address of the peer
    pub port: u16,       // Port of the peer
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum DhtValue {
    Manifest(PackageManifest),
    Peer(PeerInfo),
    // Could add Chunk data, etc. later
}

pub trait Dht {
    fn local_id(&self) -> &NodeId;
    fn store(&mut self, key: Cid, value: DhtValue);
    fn find_value(&self, key: &Cid) -> Option<DhtValue>;
    // find_peers now returns peers closest to a given target_key (could be Cid or NodeId)
    fn find_peers(&self, target_key: &NodeId, limit: usize) -> Vec<PeerInfo>;
}

pub struct InMemoryDht {
    id: NodeId,
    values: BTreeMap<Cid, DhtValue>,
    peers: Vec<PeerInfo>, // Simplified peer storage, a real Kademlia would use K-buckets
    // trust: TrustStore, // Removed for simplicity in this example, but would be here
}

impl InMemoryDht {
    pub fn new(id: NodeId) -> Self {
        Self {
            id,
            values: BTreeMap::new(),
            peers: Vec::new(),
            // trust: TrustStore::new(),
        }
    }

    pub fn add_peer(&mut self, peer: PeerInfo) {
        // In a real Kademlia, this would update K-buckets based on peer's NodeId
        self.peers.push(peer);
    }

    // Simplified method to get a random NodeId for testing
    pub fn get_random_node_id() -> NodeId {
        let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];
        // In a real system, use a cryptographically secure RNG
        for i in 0..32 { bytes[i] = i as u8; } // Dummy values
        NodeId(bytes)
    }
}

impl Dht for InMemoryDht {
    fn local_id(&self) -> &NodeId {
        &self.id
    }

    fn store(&mut self, key: Cid, value: DhtValue) {
        self.values.insert(key, value);
    }

    fn find_value(&self, key: &Cid) -> Option<DhtValue> {
        self.values.get(key).cloned()
    }

    fn find_peers(&self, target_key: &NodeId, limit: usize) -> Vec<PeerInfo> {
        let mut sorted_peers = self.peers.clone();
        // Sort peers by XOR distance to the target_key
        sorted_peers.sort_by_key(|p| p.id.distance(target_key));
        sorted_peers.truncate(limit);
        sorted_peers
    }
}

'''

# Update src/lib.rs to export the new module
rust_code_lib_rs = r'''
// src/lib.rs
#![no_std]
extern crate alloc;

pub mod cid;
pub mod manifest;
pub mod trust;
pub mod arp_dht; // New module
pub mod swarm_engine;
pub mod ipc;
pub mod syscall;
pub mod kernel;
pub mod vnode;
'''

# Print the updated code for arp_dht.rs
print(rust_code)

# Print the updated code for lib.rs
print(rust_code_lib_rs)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that `src/arp_dht.rs` is updated, the next step is to define the IPC messages for DHT lookup requests and responses that `registry-service` will use to communicate with other DHT peers over `NexusNetTransport`. This will be done by extending the `RegistryRequest` and `RegistryResponse` enums in `src/registry_ipc.rs`.


"""

print("Updating src/registry_ipc.rs with Global Search IPC messages.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/registry_ipc.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::string::String;
use alloc::vec::Vec;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::arp_dht::{NodeId, PeerInfo};
use crate::swarm_engine::global_search::{SearchRequest, SearchResponse, SearchResult};

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RegistryRequest {
    ResolveByName { name: String, version: Option<String> },
    ResolveByCid { root: Cid },
    FetchManifest { root: Cid },
    FetchPackage { root: Cid },

    FindNode { target: NodeId, limit: usize },
    FindValue { key: Cid },

    FetchChunk { cid: Cid },
    FetchPackageManifest { root_cid: Cid },

    // New variants for Global Search
    GlobalSearch(SearchRequest),
}

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RegistryResponse {
    Manifest(PackageManifest),
    Package(Vec<u8>),
    NotFound,
    Error(String),

    FoundPeers(Vec<PeerInfo>),
    FoundValue(Vec<u8>),

    ChunkData(Vec<u8>),
    ManifestData(PackageManifest),

    // New variants for Global Search
    Search(SearchResponse),
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the IPC messages for DHT lookups are defined, the next logical step is to modify the `SwarmEngine` to use these DHT lookup mechanisms when fetching manifests and chunks. This will involve updating `src/swarm_engine.rs` to integrate the `dht.find_peers` and `dht.find_value` methods.


"""

print("Updating src/swarm_engine.rs to utilize DHT lookup mechanisms.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/swarm_engine.rs

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::arp_dht::{Dht, DhtValue, PeerInfo, NodeId};
use crate::trust::{Aid, TrustStore};

#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum SwarmError {
    NotFound,
    NetworkError,
    InvalidChunk,
    NoAvailablePeers,
    IpcError,
}

pub trait SwarmTransport {
    fn fetch_chunk_from_peer(&self, peer: &PeerInfo, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError>;
}

pub struct SwarmEngine<T: SwarmTransport, D: Dht> {
    pub transport: T,
    pub dht: D,
    pub cache: BTreeMap<Cid, Vec<u8>>,
    pub trust_store: TrustStore, // Added TrustStore to SwarmEngine
    pub local_aid: Aid, // Local Aether Identity for peer reputation
}

impl<T: SwarmTransport, D: Dht> SwarmEngine<T, D> {
    pub fn new(transport: T, dht: D, trust_store: TrustStore, local_aid: Aid) -> Self {
        Self {
            transport,
            dht,
            cache: BTreeMap::new(),
            trust_store,
            local_aid,
        }
    }

    pub fn get_manifest(&mut self, root_cid: Cid) -> Result<PackageManifest, SwarmError> {
        // First try to find in local cache
        if let Some(DhtValue::Manifest(m)) = self.dht.find_value(&root_cid) {
            return Ok(m);
        }

        // If not in local cache, query DHT for peers who have this manifest
        let target_node_id = NodeId(root_cid.0); // Use CID as target for NodeId lookup
        let peers = self.dht.find_peers(&target_node_id, 8);

        if peers.is_empty() {
            return Err(SwarmError::NoAvailablePeers);
        }

        // Sort peers by reputation (higher score first)
        let mut sorted_peers = peers;
        sorted_peers.sort_by(|a, b| {
            let score_a = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&a.aid).score;
            let score_b = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&b.aid).score;
            score_b.partial_cmp(&score_a).unwrap_or(core::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
        });

        for peer in sorted_peers {
            // In a real system, this would be an IPC call to the peer for the manifest
            // For this in-memory DHT, we simulate finding the manifest value from the DHT itself.
            // A real SwarmTransport would have a method like fetch_manifest_from_peer.
            if let Some(DhtValue::Manifest(m)) = self.dht.find_value(&root_cid) { // Simplified for in-memory DHT
                // Store the manifest in local DHT cache
                self.dht.store(root_cid, DhtValue::Manifest(m.clone()));
                return Ok(m);
            }
        }

        Err(SwarmError::NotFound)
    }

    pub fn fetch_chunk(&mut self, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        // First try to find in local cache
        if let Some(data) = self.cache.get(&cid) {
            return Ok(data.clone());
        }

        // Query DHT for peers who have this chunk
        let target_node_id = NodeId(cid.0); // Use CID as target for NodeId lookup
        let peers = self.dht.find_peers(&target_node_id, 8);

        if peers.is_empty() {
            return Err(SwarmError::NoAvailablePeers);
        }

        // Sort peers by reputation (higher score first)
        let mut sorted_peers = peers;
        sorted_peers.sort_by(|a, b| {
            let score_a = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&a.aid).score;
            let score_b = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&b.aid).score;
            score_b.partial_cmp(&score_a).unwrap_or(core::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
        });

        for peer in sorted_peers {
            // Use the transport to fetch the chunk from the peer
            match self.transport.fetch_chunk_from_peer(&peer, cid) {
                Ok(data) => {
                    // Verify CID hash after fetching
                    if Cid::hash(&data) != cid {
                        log(&alloc::format!("SwarmEngine: Received invalid chunk from peer {:?} for CID {:?}", peer.id, cid));
                        // In a real system, update peer's reputation negatively
                        return Err(SwarmError::InvalidChunk);
                    }
                    self.cache.insert(cid, data.clone());
                    return Ok(data);
                },
                Err(e) => {
                    log(&alloc::format!("SwarmEngine: Failed to fetch chunk from peer {:?}: {:?}", peer.id, e));
                    // In a real system, update peer's reputation negatively
                    continue; // Try next peer
                }
            }
        }

        Err(SwarmError::NotFound)
    }

    pub fn fetch_package(&mut self, manifest: &PackageManifest) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(manifest.total_size() as usize);
        for chunk_entry in &manifest.chunks {
            let data = self.fetch_chunk(chunk_entry.cid)?; // Use the new fetch_chunk
            out.extend_from_slice(&data);
        }
        Ok(out)
    }

    // Temporary log function, should be replaced by proper V-Node logging
    fn log(msg: &str) {
        // In a real V-Node, this would use a syscall for logging
        // For now, let's just use `println!` for simulation if `std` is available,
        // or a dummy function if it's strictly `no_std`.
        // Assuming a simple `log` function is available in the V-Node context for now.
    }
}

'''

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the IPC messages for DHT lookups and the `SwarmEngine` are updated, I will define the `NexusNetTransport` struct and implement the `SwarmTransport` trait. This will allow `NexusNetTransport` to use `libnexus-net`'s `send_to` and `recv` methods for fetching chunks over UDP, completing the implementation of a real network transport.


"""

print("Creating src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs to implement SwarmTransport.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmTransport, SwarmError};
use crate::arp_dht::PeerInfo;
use libnexus_net::{NetClient, NetError};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

pub struct NexusNetTransport {
    net_client: NetClient,
    udp_socket_handle: u32, // Re-use a single UDP socket for all fetches
}

impl NexusNetTransport {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, NetError> {
        let mut net_client = NetClient::new();
        let udp_socket_handle = net_client.open_udp_socket(0)?; // Open an ephemeral UDP socket
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Opened UDP socket with handle: {}", udp_socket_handle));
        Ok(NexusNetTransport {
            net_client,
            udp_socket_handle,
        })
    }
}

impl SwarmTransport for NexusNetTransport {
    fn fetch_chunk_from_peer(&self, peer: &PeerInfo, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Fetching chunk {} from peer {}:{}",
            alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes()), peer.ip_address[0], peer.port));

        // Serialize CID for sending
        let request_payload = postcard::to_allocvec(&cid).map_err(|_| SwarmError::NetworkError)?; // Change to SwarmError

        // Send CID request to the peer over UDP
        self.net_client.send_to(
            self.udp_socket_handle,
            peer.ip_address,
            peer.port,
            request_payload
        ).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to send request: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // Receive the response (chunk data)
        // This will block until a response is received or a timeout occurs
        // In a real system, we'd have a more robust async receive with timeouts
        let response_payload = self.net_client.recv(self.udp_socket_handle).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to receive response: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // In a real scenario, the response payload would be verified and parsed to extract the chunk data.
        // For this sketch, we assume the response_payload IS the chunk data.
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Received {} bytes for chunk {}", response_payload.len(), alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes())));
        Ok(response_payload)
    }
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that `NexusNetTransport` is defined, I will update `vnode/registry/src/main.rs` to integrate it. This involves replacing `FakeTransport` with `NexusNetTransport` when initializing `SwarmEngine`. I also need to update `src/lib.rs` to export `nexus_net_transport` from `swarm_engine`.


"""

print("Updating vnode/registry/src/main.rs to integrate NexusNetTransport.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;
use core::panic::PanicInfo;

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::registry_service::RegistryService;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmEngine, SwarmTransport};
use crate::arp_dht::{InMemoryDht, PeerInfo, NodeId};
use crate::trust::{TrustStore, Aid};

// Import NexusNetTransport
use crate::swarm_engine::nexus_net_transport::NexusNetTransport;

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes (redefined for registry to avoid module conflict)
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error */ }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // Net-bridge's own channel ID (to receive IRQ events from kernel)
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(1); // Channel for RegistryService's IPC

    log("Registry V-Node starting up...");

    // 1. Initialize NexusNetTransport (which uses NetClient internally)
    let transport = match NexusNetTransport::new() {
        Ok(t) => {
            log("Registry: NexusNetTransport initialized.");
            t
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to initialize NexusNetTransport: {:?}", e));
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    // --- Swarm Engine Initialization ---
    let trust_store = TrustStore::new();
    let local_aid = Aid([0xCD; 32]); // Dummy local AID
    let mut dht = InMemoryDht::new(NodeId([0; 32])); // Initialize DHT with a dummy NodeId

    // Add some dummy peers for DHT to find
    dht.add_peer(PeerInfo {
        id: NodeId([0xAA; 32]),
        aid: crate::trust::Aid([0xBB; 32]),
        ip_address: [10, 0, 2, 1], // Example peer IP
        port: 60000, // Example peer port
    });

    // Load a dummy package for demonstration
    let (manifest, chunks) = crate::examples::hello_package::make_hello_package();
    dht.store(manifest.root_cid, crate::arp_dht::DhtValue::Manifest(manifest.clone()));

    let mut swarm = SwarmEngine::new(transport, dht, trust_store, local_aid);
    // --- End Swarm Engine Initialization ---

    // Simulate fetching a package from the swarm using the real network transport
    match swarm.fetch_package(&manifest) {
        Ok(data) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Successfully fetched package '{}' ({} bytes)", manifest.name, data.len()));
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to fetch package: {:?}", e));
        }
    }

    loop {
        // Original RegistryService loop logic would go here, handling IPC requests
        // For now, we simulate just logging to avoid complexity
        log("Registry V-Node idling...");
        // In a real V-Node, it would be waiting for IPC messages
        let _ = own_chan.recv_blocking(); // Block and wait for IPC messages
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Registry V-Node panicked!");
    loop {} // Keep looping in case of panic
}
'''

print(rust_code)

// src/registry_ipc.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::string::String;
use alloc::vec::Vec;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::arp_dht::{NodeId, PeerInfo};

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RegistryRequest {
    ResolveByName { name: String, version: Option<String> },
    ResolveByCid { root: Cid },
    FetchManifest { root: Cid },
    FetchPackage { root: Cid },

    // New DHT-related requests
    FindNode { target: NodeId, limit: usize },
    FindValue { key: Cid },

    // New variants for fetching chunks and manifests directly
    FetchChunk { cid: Cid },
    FetchPackageManifest { root_cid: Cid },
}

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RegistryResponse {
    Manifest(PackageManifest),
    Package(Vec<u8>),
    NotFound,
    Error(String),

    // New DHT-related responses
    FoundPeers(Vec<PeerInfo>),
    FoundValue(Vec<u8>), // Could be manifest bytes or chunk data

    // New variants for returning chunk and manifest data
    ChunkData(Vec<u8>),
    ManifestData(PackageManifest),
}
'''

## Define Sync Root & Sync Daemon

### Subtask:
Define the structure and role of the Sync Root directory within AetherFS and the Sync Daemon V-Node responsible for managing data synchronization.

## 🌳 The Sync Root: Your Personal Cloud Gateway

In AetherOS, the **Sync Root** is a designated directory within a user's **AetherFS** `/home` space that is explicitly marked for **peer-to-peer synchronization** across all of their **AID-verified devices**. It acts as the local endpoint for **Aether Cloud Sync** (from Stage 5, Step 4).

### 1. Location and Structure

*   **Path**: By default, the Sync Root will be located at `/home/<AID>/sync`. Each user's unique Aether Identity (AID) ensures proper isolation and ownership of their synchronized data.
*   **Creation**: It is automatically created and managed by the `svc://sync-daemon` V-Node upon user login or explicit configuration.
*   **Mount Point**: While it appears as a regular directory to the user, it's an intelligent mount point that `AetherFS` uses to route filesystem events to the `sync-daemon`.

### 2. Core Principles

*   **AID-Controlled**: Access to the Sync Root and its contents is strictly controlled by the user's AID. Only devices authenticated with the same AID can access and synchronize its data.
*   **Zero-Trust by Design**: Data within the Sync Root is always **end-to-end encrypted** using keys derived from the user's AID, even when stored locally or in transit. The `sync-daemon` does not have access to unencrypted data.
*   **Event-Driven**: The `sync-daemon` monitors the Sync Root for filesystem events (create, modify, delete) through an **AetherFS API**. These events trigger synchronization actions.
*   **Chunked & Deduplicated**: Data within the Sync Root is automatically **chunked and content-addressed (CID)**. This means only changed chunks are transferred, and redundant data across devices is deduplicated, saving bandwidth and storage.

## ⚙️ The Sync Daemon V-Node (`svc://sync-daemon`)

The `svc://sync-daemon` is a critical system **V-Node** responsible for managing the end-to-end synchronization process for the user's Sync Root. It operates in user-space, communicating with `AetherFS`, `AetherCrypto Service`, and the `Swarm Engine` via IPC to fulfill its duties.

### 1. Primary Responsibilities

*   **Filesystem Event Monitoring**: The daemon registers with `AetherFS` to receive real-time notifications about changes (creations, modifications, deletions) within the user's Sync Root directory. This is done via an IPC-based **AetherFS API**.
*   **Data Chunking & CID Generation**: Upon detecting a change, the `sync-daemon` reads the affected file/data, **chunks** it into fixed-size blocks, and computes the **Content ID (CID)** for each chunk. This process is optimized for efficiency and leverages memory-safe operations.
*   **Merkle-DAG Construction**: For every file or directory, the daemon maintains or reconstructs its **Merkle-DAG**. This allows for efficient detection of changes and cryptographic verification of data integrity.
*   **Interaction with Swarm Engine**: It utilizes the `Swarm Engine` (from Stage 5, Step 5) to:
    *   **Publish Data**: Announces the availability of new or updated chunks (by their CIDs) to the global DHT, allowing other AID-verified devices to discover and download them.
    *   **Fetch Data**: Actively queries the DHT for chunks that are missing or outdated on the local device, initiating P2P transfers via the `Swarm Engine`'s transport layer.
*   **Synchronization Coordination**: Manages the state of synchronization, tracks progress, and resolves basic conflicts (e.g., Last-Write-Wins for simple files) if multiple devices modify the same data concurrently.
*   **Cryptographic Operations**: Collaborates with the `AetherCrypto Service` (from Stage 4, Step 8) to encrypt and decrypt chunks. The `sync-daemon` never directly handles the user's private keys but sends data for encryption/decryption requests.
*   **Resource Management**: Monitors its own resource consumption and adheres to its declared resource quotas, ensuring it does not negatively impact system performance.

### 2. Operational Flow (High-Level)

1.  **Initialization**: Upon user login or activation, `sync-daemon` initializes its connection with `AetherFS`, `AetherCrypto Service`, and the `Swarm Engine`.
2.  **Sync Root Registration**: It registers the user's Sync Root path (`/home/<AID>/sync`) with `AetherFS`, requesting to be notified of relevant filesystem events.
3.  **Local Scan**: Performs an initial scan of the Sync Root to build its local Merkle-DAG and compare it with the expected state from other devices.
4.  **Event Loop**: Enters a continuous loop, responding to:
    *   **Filesystem Events (from AetherFS)**: Processes local changes, chunks new data, updates Merkle-DAGs, and publishes CIDs to the Swarm.
    *   **Swarm Events (from Swarm Engine)**: Receives notifications about available remote chunks, fetches them, decrypts them, and applies them to the local Sync Root.
    *   **IPC Queries (from Dashboard/CLI)**: Provides sync status, progress, and historical data.

**Reasoning**:
Now that the Sync Root concept and the Sync Daemon's responsibilities are outlined, the next step is to specify the initial set of capabilities required by the `sync-daemon` V-Node in its `vnode.yml` manifest, as per the subtask instruction.
"""

print("```yaml\n# vnode/sync-daemon/vnode.yml\nvnode:\n  name: \"sync-daemon\"\n  version: \"0.1.0\"\n  maintainer: \"aetheros-core-team@aetheros.org\"\n  mode: strict # A system daemon for data integrity\n\nruntime:\n  entrypoint: \"bin/sync-daemon.vnode\"\n  required_mem_mb: 32 # For chunking buffers, Merkle-DAG state, and P2P connections\n  max_cpu_share: 0.10 # Can be bursty depending on file changes and network activity\n\ncapabilities:\n  - StorageAccess: \"/home/<AID>/sync\" # Access to the user's Sync Root for monitoring and modification\n  - CAP_IPC_CONNECT: \"svc://aetherfs\" # To register for filesystem events and perform FS operations\n  - CAP_IPC_CONNECT: \"svc://crypto\" # To request encryption/decryption of data chunks\n  - CAP_IPC_CONNECT: \"svc://swarm\" # To interact with the Swarm Engine for data distribution\n  - NetworkAccess # For Swarm Engine's underlying network operations\n  - CAP_TIME_READ # For timestamping events and synchronization logic\n  - CAP_IPC_ACCEPT # To accept status queries from Dashboard/CLI\n  - CAP_LOG_WRITE # To log synchronization events and errors\n\nstorage:\n  mounts:\n    - path: \"/home/<AID>/sync\"\n      source: \"aetherfs://user/<AID>/sync\"\n      options: [ \"rw\", \"monitor\" ] # Read/write access, plus event monitoring\n    - path: \"/var/sync-state\"\n      source: \"persistent://system-state/sync-daemon\"\n      options: [ \"rw\" ] # To store synchronization state, Merkle-DAGs, etc.\n\nobservability:\n  metrics: [\"files_synced_total\", \"bytes_synced_total\", \"sync_latency_avg_ms\", \"sync_conflicts_total\", \"swarm_peers_connected\"]\n```")

"""### 3. IPC API for `svc://sync-daemon`

To fulfill its responsibilities, the `sync-daemon` will expose and consume several IPC messages via its dedicated channel (e.g., channel ID 5, resolved via Aether Name Service).

*   **`REGISTER_SYNC_ROOT` (from AetherFS)**:
    *   **Purpose**: AetherFS uses this message to notify the `sync-daemon` that a user's Sync Root is mounted and ready for monitoring. It can also be used by AetherFS to push filesystem events.
    *   **Payload**: `SyncRootRegistration { user_aid: Aid, path: String, fs_event_channel: ChannelId }`

*   **`SYNC_STATUS_QUERY` (from Dashboard/CLI)**:
    *   **Purpose**: Allows external tools (like the Nexus Dashboard or `ash` CLI) to query the current synchronization status of the user's Sync Root.
    *   **Payload**: `SyncStatusRequest { user_aid: Aid }`
    *   **Response**: `SyncStatusResponse { total_files: u32, pending_files: u32, last_sync_timestamp: u64, conflicts: Vec<String> }`

*   **`DATA_SYNC_EVENT` (from Swarm Engine)**:
    *   **Purpose**: The `Swarm Engine` uses this message to notify `sync-daemon` about relevant events related to data chunks (e.g., a new chunk for a file in the Sync Root has been discovered or fetched).
    *   **Payload**: `SwarmDataEvent { chunk_cid: Cid, event_type: SyncEventType }`
        *   `SyncEventType`: `ChunkAvailable`, `ChunkFetched`, `ChunkPublished`

*   **`ENCRYPT_CHUNK_REQUEST` / `DECRYPT_CHUNK_REQUEST` (to AetherCrypto Service)**:
    *   **Purpose**: `sync-daemon` will send these messages to `svc://crypto` to handle cryptographic operations on data chunks, ensuring private keys never leave the secure Vault.
    *   **Payload**: `CryptoRequest { operation: CryptoOp, chunk_data_handle: u64, key_handle: u64 }`

This IPC API defines the interaction points, ensuring all communications are explicit, capability-checked, and isolated within the AetherOS microkernel architecture.

## Integrate Swarm Engine for Data Exchange

### Subtask:
Detail how the `Sync Daemon` V-Node will use the `Swarm Engine` for P2P exchange of encrypted, chunked data between AID-verified devices.

**Reasoning**:
First, I need to define the IPC message formats (`SwarmRequest` and `SwarmResponse`) that `sync-daemon` will use to communicate with `svc://swarm`. These messages will encapsulate requests for publishing, fetching, and querying chunks. I will define these in a new module, `src/ipc/swarm_ipc.rs`.
"""

print("Creating src/ipc/swarm_ipc.rs to define the IPC message formats for Swarm Engine communication.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/ipc/swarm_ipc.rs

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::string::String;

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest; // Assuming manifest can be part of swarm messages
use crate::arp_dht::{PeerInfo, NodeId}; // Assuming these can be part of swarm messages

/// Represents requests from V-Nodes (e.g., sync-daemon) to the svc://swarm V-Node.
#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum SwarmRequest {
    /// Request to publish a chunk to the DHT.
    PublishChunk { cid: Cid, data: Vec<u8> },
    /// Request to publish a manifest to the DHT.
    PublishManifest { manifest: PackageManifest },
    /// Request to fetch a specific chunk by CID.
    FetchChunk { cid: Cid },
    /// Request to query the DHT for manifest CIDs related to a user's Sync Root (e.g., by AID).
    QuerySyncRootManifests { user_aid: [u8; 32] },
    /// Request to find peers for a given CID.
    FindPeers { cid: Cid, limit: usize },
    /// Request to find a specific value (e.g., Manifest) in the DHT.
    FindValue { key: Cid },
}

/// Represents responses from the svc://swarm V-Node to client V-Nodes.
#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum SwarmResponse {
    /// Indicates successful operation.
    Success,
    /// Returns the data for a requested chunk.
    ChunkData(Vec<u8>),
    /// Returns a list of manifests for a Sync Root query.
    SyncRootManifests(Vec<PackageManifest>),
    /// Returns a list of PeerInfo.
    FoundPeers(Vec<PeerInfo>),
    /// Returns a specific DhtValue.
    FoundValue(Vec<u8>),
    /// Indicates that the requested item was not found.
    NotFound,
    /// Indicates an error occurred during the operation.
    Error(String),
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the IPC message formats for `svc://swarm` are defined, I need to sketch the Rust code for the `sync-daemon` V-Node (`vnode/sync-daemon/main.rs`). This code will demonstrate its main event loop and conceptual interactions with `svc://swarm`, `svc://aetherfs`, and `svc://crypto`, as well as handle IPC requests for data synchronization operations.


"""

print("Creating vnode/sync-daemon/main.rs to sketch the Sync Daemon V-Node.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::string::{String, ToString};
use alloc::format;

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall3, SYS_LOG, SUCCESS, SYS_TIME}; // Assuming SYS_TIME for timestamps
use crate::ipc::swarm_ipc::{SwarmRequest, SwarmResponse}; // IPC with svc://swarm

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest; // For representing Sync Root manifests
use crate::trust::Aid; // For user identity

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// Placeholder for AetherFS IPC API
#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
enum AetherFsRequest {
    RegisterSyncRootMonitor { user_aid: Aid, path: String, fs_event_channel: u32 },
    ReadChunk { path: String, offset: u64, len: u32 },
    WriteChunk { path: String, offset: u64, data: Vec<u8> },
    // ... other FS operations
}

#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
enum AetherFsResponse {
    SyncRootRegistered,
    ChunkData(Vec<u8>),
    FsEvent(FsEvent),
    Error(String),
}

#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
enum FsEvent {
    FileCreated(String),
    FileModified(String),
    FileDeleted(String),
}

// Placeholder for AetherCrypto Service IPC API
#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
enum CryptoRequest {
    EncryptChunk { dma_handle: u64, key_handle: u64 },
    DecryptChunk { dma_handle: u64, key_handle: u64 },
}

#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
enum CryptoResponse {
    EncryptedData { dma_handle: u64, len: u64 },
    DecryptedData { dma_handle: u64, len: u64 },
    Error(String),
}

// --- Sync Daemon Main Loop ---
#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // Channel for requests from Dashboard/CLI (SyncStatusQuery)
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(5); // Assuming channel ID 5 for sync-daemon

    // Channel to communicate with AetherFS Service
    let mut aetherfs_chan = VNodeChannel::new(6); // Assuming channel ID 6 for AetherFS

    // Channel to communicate with AetherCrypto Service
    let mut crypto_chan = VNodeChannel::new(7); // Assuming channel ID 7 for AetherCrypto

    // Channel to communicate with Swarm Engine
    let mut swarm_chan = VNodeChannel::new(8); // Assuming channel ID 8 for Swarm Engine

    log("Sync Daemon V-Node starting up...");

    // Dummy AID for the local user
    let local_user_aid = Aid([0xDE; 32]);
    let sync_root_path = format!("/home/{:?}/sync", local_user_aid.0);

    // 1. Register Sync Root with AetherFS for event monitoring
    log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Registering sync root {}", sync_root_path));
    let fs_req = AetherFsRequest::RegisterSyncRootMonitor {
        user_aid: local_user_aid,
        path: sync_root_path.clone(),
        fs_event_channel: own_chan.id,
    };
    aetherfs_chan.send(&fs_req).unwrap_or_else(|_| log("Sync Daemon: Failed to register with AetherFS."));
    // In a real system, we'd wait for a response here

    log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Monitoring sync root: {}", sync_root_path));

    // Main event loop for the Sync Daemon
    loop {
        // 1. Process incoming filesystem events from AetherFS
        if let Ok(Some(fs_msg_data)) = own_chan.recv_non_blocking() {
            if let Ok(fs_resp) = postcard::from_bytes::<AetherFsResponse>(&fs_msg_data) {
                match fs_resp {
                    AetherFsResponse::FsEvent(event) => {
                        log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Received FS event: {:?}", event));
                        // a. Detect local filesystem changes
                        // b. Initiate chunking and CID generation for changed data (conceptual)
                        let file_path = match event {
                            FsEvent::FileCreated(p) => p,
                            FsEvent::FileModified(p) => p,
                            FsEvent::FileDeleted(p) => p,
                        };
                        log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Processing file change for {}", file_path));

                        // For demonstration, let's say we have a dummy chunk and CID
                        let dummy_data = b"dummy sync data for file ".to_vec();
                        let dummy_cid = Cid::hash(&dummy_data);

                        // c. Send IPC requests to svc://swarm to publish new chunks to the DHT
                        let publish_req = SwarmRequest::PublishChunk { cid: dummy_cid, data: dummy_data.clone() };
                        swarm_chan.send(&publish_req).unwrap_or_else(|_| log("Sync Daemon: Failed to publish chunk to Swarm."));
                        log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Published dummy chunk {:?} to Swarm.", dummy_cid));

                        // In a real system, also publish manifest or update Merkle-DAG

                    },
                    _ => log("Sync Daemon: Received unexpected AetherFS response."),
                }
            } else {
                log("Sync Daemon: Failed to deserialize AetherFSResponse.");
            }
        }

        // 2. Process incoming swarm events (e.g., notification about remote chunks)
        if let Ok(Some(swarm_msg_data)) = swarm_chan.recv_non_blocking() {
            if let Ok(swarm_resp) = postcard::from_bytes::<SwarmResponse>(&swarm_msg_data) {
                match swarm_resp {
                    SwarmResponse::ChunkData(data) => {
                        let cid = Cid::hash(&data);
                        log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Received chunk data {:?} from Swarm.", cid));
                        // e. Process fetched data (decrypt, write to AetherFS)
                        // For now, conceptual
                        log("Sync Daemon: (Conceptual) Decrypting and writing chunk to AetherFS.");
                    },
                    SwarmResponse::FoundPeers(peers) => {
                        log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Found {} peers for a query.", peers.len()));
                        // d. Initiate fetching missing/outdated chunks
                        // (conceptual: would iterate over needed chunks and request them)
                        let dummy_cid_to_fetch = Cid::hash(b"some missing data");
                        let fetch_req = SwarmRequest::FetchChunk { cid: dummy_cid_to_fetch };
                        swarm_chan.send(&fetch_req).unwrap_or_else(|_| log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Failed to fetch chunk {:?} from Swarm.", dummy_cid_to_fetch)));
                        log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Requested dummy chunk {:?}.", dummy_cid_to_fetch));

                    },
                    _ => log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Received unexpected SwarmResponse: {:?}", swarm_resp)),
                }
            } else {
                log("Sync Daemon: Failed to deserialize SwarmResponse.");
            }
        }

        // 3. Process requests from Dashboard/CLI for sync status
        // (conceptual, similar to how other V-Nodes handle requests)
        if let Ok(Some(cli_req_data)) = own_chan.recv_non_blocking() {
            log(&format!("Sync Daemon: Received CLI/Dashboard request: {:?}", cli_req_data));
            // ... handle SyncStatusRequest and send SyncStatusResponse
        }

        // Use SYS_TIME to allow other V-Nodes to run while this one is checking for events
        unsafe { syscall3(SYS_TIME, 0, 0, 0); }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Sync Daemon V-Node panicked!");
    loop {}
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the Sync Root concept and the Sync Daemon's responsibilities are outlined, the next step is to specify the initial set of capabilities required by the `sync-daemon` V-Node in its `vnode.yml` manifest, as per the subtask instruction.


"""

print("```yaml\n# vnode/sync-daemon/vnode.yml\nvnode:\n  name: \"sync-daemon\"\n  version: \"0.1.0\"\n  maintainer: \"aetheros-core-team@aetheros.org\"\n  mode: strict # A system daemon for data integrity\n\nruntime:\n  entrypoint: \"bin/sync-daemon.vnode\"\n  required_mem_mb: 32 # For chunking buffers, Merkle-DAG state, and P2P connections\n  max_cpu_share: 0.10 # Can be bursty depending on file changes and network activity\n\ncapabilities:\n  - StorageAccess: \"/home/<AID>/sync\" # Access to the user's Sync Root for monitoring and modification\n  - CAP_IPC_CONNECT: \"svc://aetherfs\" # To register for filesystem events and perform FS operations\n  - CAP_IPC_CONNECT: \"svc://crypto\" # To request encryption/decryption of data chunks\n  - CAP_IPC_CONNECT: \"svc://swarm\" # To interact with the Swarm Engine for data distribution\n  - NetworkAccess # For Swarm Engine's underlying network operations\n  - CAP_TIME_READ # For timestamping events and synchronization logic\n  - CAP_IPC_ACCEPT # To accept status queries from Dashboard/CLI\n  - CAP_LOG_WRITE # To log synchronization events and errors\n\nstorage:\n  mounts:\n    - path: \"/home/<AID>/sync\"\n      source: \"aetherfs://user/<AID>/sync\"\n      options: [ \"rw\", \"monitor\" ] # Read/write access, plus event monitoring\n    - path: \"/var/sync-state\"\n      source: \"persistent://system-state/sync-daemon\"\n      options: [ \"rw\" ] # To store synchronization state, Merkle-DAGs, etc.\n\nobservability:\n  metrics: [\"files_synced_total\", \"bytes_synced_total\", \"sync_latency_avg_ms\", \"sync_conflicts_total\", \"swarm_peers_connected\"]\n```")

            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );

// --- Sync Daemon Main Loop ---

        if let Ok(Some(swarm_msg_data)) = swarm_chan.recv_non_blocking() {

"""**Reasoning**:
To properly integrate `NexusNetTransport` into the `registry-service` V-Node, the `nexus_net_transport` module must be exposed from the `swarm_engine` crate. I will update `src/lib.rs` to export this module.

// src/lib.rs

extern crate alloc;

pub mod cid;
pub mod manifest;
pub mod trust;

print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
The `nexus_net_transport` module needs to be publicly re-exported from the `nexus-core` crate's root (`src/lib.rs`) to be easily accessible by other crates like `libnexus-net` or `vnode/registry` that might refer to it directly. This `pub use` statement makes it available as `nexus_core::nexus_net_transport`.


"""

print("Updating src/lib.rs to export `nexus_net_transport`.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/lib.rs
#![no_std]
extern crate alloc;

pub mod cid;
pub mod manifest;
pub mod trust;
pub mod arp_dht;
pub mod swarm_engine;
pub use swarm_engine::nexus_net_transport; // Re-export NexusNetTransport module for direct access
pub mod ipc;
pub mod syscall;
pub mod kernel;
pub mod vnode;
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.

"""## Integrate Networking into Registry Service

### Subtask:
Integrate the `NexusNetTransport` into the `registry-service` V-Node.

**Reasoning**:
The subtask requires integrating `NexusNetTransport` into the `registry-service` V-Node. This involves updating `vnode/registry/src/main.rs` to replace `FakeTransport` with `NexusNetTransport` during the initialization of `SwarmEngine`, ensuring all necessary `use` statements are present, and correctly passing `TrustStore` and `Aid` to `SwarmEngine::new`.
"""

print("Updating vnode/registry/src/main.rs to integrate NexusNetTransport and GlobalSearchService.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;
use core::panic::PanicInfo;

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::registry_service::RegistryService;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmEngine, SwarmTransport};
use crate::arp_dht::{InMemoryDht, PeerInfo, NodeId};
use crate::trust::{TrustStore, Aid};

// Import NexusNetTransport
use crate::swarm_engine::nexus_net_transport::NexusNetTransport;
use crate::swarm_engine::global_search::GlobalSearchService; // Import GlobalSearchService

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes (redefined for registry to avoid module conflict)
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error */ }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // Net-bridge's own channel ID (to receive IRQ events from kernel)
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(1); // Channel for RegistryService's IPC

    log("Registry V-Node starting up...");

    // 1. Initialize NexusNetTransport (which uses NetClient internally)
    let transport = match NexusNetTransport::new() {
        Ok(t) => {
            log("Registry: NexusNetTransport initialized.");
            t
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to initialize NexusNetTransport: {:?}", e));
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    // --- Swarm Engine Initialization ---
    let trust_store = TrustStore::new();
    let local_aid = Aid([0xCD; 32]); // Dummy local AID
    let mut dht = InMemoryDht::new(NodeId([0; 32])); // Initialize DHT with a dummy NodeId

    // Add some dummy peers for DHT to find
    dht.add_peer(PeerInfo {
        id: NodeId([0xAA; 32]),
        aid: crate::trust::Aid([0xBB; 32]),
        ip_address: [10, 0, 2, 1], // Example peer IP
        port: 60000, // Example peer port
    });

    // Load a dummy package for demonstration
    let (manifest, chunks) = crate::examples::hello_package::make_hello_package();
    dht.store(manifest.root_cid, crate::arp_dht::DhtValue::Manifest(manifest.clone()));

    let global_search_service = GlobalSearchService::new(dht, trust_store, local_aid); // Initialize GlobalSearchService
    // Note: Dht is moved into GlobalSearchService, you might need to clone or pass a reference if both need it.
    // For this example, we'll clone Dht if needed, or re-create it for swarm_engine if it takes ownership.

    let mut dht_for_swarm = InMemoryDht::new(NodeId([0; 32])); // Re-initialize DHT for SwarmEngine if GlobalSearchService takes ownership
    // Re-add dummy peer info for dht_for_swarm if needed
    dht_for_swarm.add_peer(PeerInfo {
        id: NodeId([0xAA; 32]),
        aid: crate::trust::Aid([0xBB; 32]),
        ip_address: [10, 0, 2, 1], // Example peer IP
        port: 60000, // Example peer port
    });
    dht_for_swarm.store(manifest.root_cid, crate::arp_dht::DhtValue::Manifest(manifest.clone()));


    let mut swarm = SwarmEngine::new(transport, dht_for_swarm, trust_store, local_aid); // Pass trust_store and local_aid
    // --- End Swarm Engine Initialization ---

    // Simulate fetching a package from the swarm using the real network transport
    match swarm.fetch_package(&manifest) {
        Ok(data) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Successfully fetched package '{}' ({} bytes)", manifest.name, data.len()));
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to fetch package: {:?}", e));
        }
    }

    // Test Global Search
    let search_request = crate::swarm_engine::global_search::SearchRequest::KeywordSearch { query: String::from("hello") };
    let search_response = global_search_service.handle_search_request(search_request);
    log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Global Search Response: {:?}", search_response));


    loop {
        // Original RegistryService loop logic would go here, handling IPC requests
        // For now, we simulate just logging to avoid complexity
        log("Registry V-Node idling...");
        // In a real V-Node, it would be waiting for IPC messages
        let _ = own_chan.recv_blocking(); // Block and wait for IPC messages
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
pub extern "C" fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Registry V-Node panicked!");
    loop {} // Keep looping in case of panic
}
'''

print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
The subtask requires integrating `NexusNetTransport` into the `registry-service` V-Node. This involves updating `vnode/registry/src/main.rs` to replace `FakeTransport` with `NexusNetTransport` during the initialization of `SwarmEngine`, ensuring all necessary `use` statements are present, and correctly passing `TrustStore` and `Aid` to `SwarmEngine::new`. The previous code had a logical issue where `dht`, `trust_store`, and `local_aid` were moved into `GlobalSearchService` preventing their reuse for `SwarmEngine`. This update clones these values for proper initialization of both services.

#![no_std]

use core::panic::PanicInfo;

    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );

#[no_mangle]

    log("Registry V-Node starting up...");

    // 1. Initialize NexusNetTransport (which uses NetClient internally)

        Ok(t) => {

            t

            log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Failed to initialize NexusNetTransport: {:?}", e));
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }

    // --- Swarm Engine Initialization ---

    let local_aid = Aid([0xCD; 32]); // Dummy local AID
    let local_node_id = NodeId([0; 32]); // Dummy NodeId for local DHT

    let mut dht_for_init = InMemoryDht::new(local_node_id.clone()); // Use a clone for initial setup

    // Add some dummy peers for DHT to find
    dht_for_init.add_peer(PeerInfo {
        id: NodeId([0xAA; 32]),
        aid: crate::trust::Aid([0xBB; 32]),
        ip_address: [10, 0, 2, 1], // Example peer IP
        port: 60000, // Example peer port
    });

    dht_for_init.store(manifest.root_cid, crate::arp_dht::DhtValue::Manifest(manifest.clone()));

        }

    let search_response = global_search_service.handle_search_request(search_request);
    log(&alloc::format!("Registry: Global Search Response: {:?}", search_response));


    loop {
        // Original RegistryService loop logic would go here, handling IPC requests
        // For now, we simulate just logging to avoid complexity
        log("Registry V-Node idling...");
        // In a real V-Node, it would be waiting for IPC messages
