"""## Implement DHT Keyspace & NodeID Routing

### Subtask:
Implement the DHT Keyspace and NodeID routing for the Aether Registry Protocol (ARP) in the `registry-service` V-Node.

**Reasoning**:
The subtask is to implement DHT Keyspace and NodeID routing. This requires defining the NodeId, its distance calculation using XOR, and updating the InMemoryDht and PeerInfo structures to use these. I will provide the updated `src/arp_dht.rs` code, along with `src/lib.rs` to ensure the new module is exported.
"""

print("Updating src/arp_dht.rs for DHT Keyspace and NodeID routing.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/arp_dht.rs
#![allow(dead_code)]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::trust::Aid; // Assuming Aid is defined in trust.rs

// A 256-bit Node ID for Kademlia-like DHT
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]
pub struct NodeId(pub [u8; 32]);

impl NodeId {
    /// Calculates the XOR distance between two NodeIds.
    pub fn distance(&self, other: &NodeId) -> [u8; 32] {
        let mut dist = [0u8; 32];
        for i in 0..32 {
            dist[i] = self.0[i] ^ other.0[i];
        }
        dist
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct PeerInfo {
    pub id: NodeId,     // The NodeId of the peer
    pub aid: Aid,       // The Aether Identity of the peer
    pub ip_address: [u8; 4], // IP address of the peer
    pub port: u16,       // Port of the peer
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum DhtValue {
    Manifest(PackageManifest),
    Peer(PeerInfo),
    // Could add Chunk data, etc. later
}

pub trait Dht {
    fn local_id(&self) -> &NodeId;
    fn store(&mut self, key: Cid, value: DhtValue);
    fn find_value(&self, key: &Cid) -> Option<DhtValue>;
    // find_peers now returns peers closest to a given target_key (could be Cid or NodeId)
    fn find_peers(&self, target_key: &NodeId, limit: usize) -> Vec<PeerInfo>;
}

pub struct InMemoryDht {
    id: NodeId,
    values: BTreeMap<Cid, DhtValue>,
    peers: Vec<PeerInfo>, // Simplified peer storage, a real Kademlia would use K-buckets
    // trust: TrustStore, // Removed for simplicity in this example, but would be here
}

impl InMemoryDht {
    pub fn new(id: NodeId) -> Self {
        Self {
            id,
            values: BTreeMap::new(),
            peers: Vec::new(),
            // trust: TrustStore::new(),
        }
    }

    pub fn add_peer(&mut self, peer: PeerInfo) {
        // In a real Kademlia, this would update K-buckets based on peer's NodeId
        self.peers.push(peer);
    }

    // Simplified method to get a random NodeId for testing
    pub fn get_random_node_id() -> NodeId {
        let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];
        // In a real system, use a cryptographically secure RNG
        for i in 0..32 { bytes[i] = i as u8; } // Dummy values
        NodeId(bytes)
    }
}

impl Dht for InMemoryDht {
    fn local_id(&self) -> &NodeId {
        &self.id
    }

    fn store(&mut self, key: Cid, value: DhtValue) {
        self.values.insert(key, value);
    }

    fn find_value(&self, key: &Cid) -> Option<DhtValue> {
        self.values.get(key).cloned()
    }

    fn find_peers(&self, target_key: &NodeId, limit: usize) -> Vec<PeerInfo> {
        let mut sorted_peers = self.peers.clone();
        // Sort peers by XOR distance to the target_key
        sorted_peers.sort_by_key(|p| p.id.distance(target_key));
        sorted_peers.truncate(limit);
        sorted_peers
    }
}

'''

# Update src/lib.rs to export the new module
rust_code_lib_rs = r'''
// src/lib.rs
#![no_std]
extern crate alloc;

pub mod cid;
pub mod manifest;
pub mod trust;
pub mod arp_dht; // New module
pub mod swarm_engine;
pub mod ipc;
pub mod syscall;
pub mod kernel;
pub mod vnode;
'''

# Print the updated code for arp_dht.rs
print(rust_code)

# Print the updated code for lib.rs
print(rust_code_lib_rs)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the `src/arp_dht.rs` is updated, the next step is to define the IPC messages for DHT lookup requests and responses that `registry-service` will use to communicate with other DHT peers over `NexusNetTransport`. This will be done by extending the `RegistryRequest` and `RegistryResponse` enums in `src/registry_ipc.rs`.


"""

print("Updating src/registry_ipc.rs with Global Search IPC messages.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/registry_ipc.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::string::String;
use alloc::vec::Vec;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::arp_dht::{NodeId, PeerInfo};
use crate::swarm_engine::global_search::{SearchRequest, SearchResponse, SearchResult};

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RegistryRequest {
    ResolveByName { name: String, version: Option<String> },
    ResolveByCid { root: Cid },
    FetchManifest { root: Cid },
    FetchPackage { root: Cid },

    // New DHT-related requests
    FindNode { target: NodeId, limit: usize },
    FindValue { key: Cid },

    FetchChunk { cid: Cid },
    FetchPackageManifest { root_cid: Cid },

    // New variants for Global Search
    GlobalSearch(SearchRequest),
}

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RegistryResponse {
    Manifest(PackageManifest),
    Package(Vec<u8>),
    NotFound,
    Error(String),

    // New DHT-related responses
    FoundPeers(Vec<PeerInfo>),
    FoundValue(Vec<u8>),

    // New variants for returning chunk and manifest data
    ChunkData(Vec<u8>),
    ManifestData(PackageManifest),

    // New variants for Global Search
    Search(SearchResponse),
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the IPC messages for DHT lookups and the `SwarmEngine` are updated, I will define the `NexusNetTransport` struct and implement the `SwarmTransport` trait. This will allow `NexusNetTransport` to use `libnexus-net`'s `send_to` and `recv` methods for fetching chunks over UDP, completing the implementation of a real network transport.


"""

print("Creating src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs to implement SwarmTransport.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmTransport, SwarmError};
use crate::arp_dht::PeerInfo;
use libnexus_net::{NetClient, NetError};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

pub struct NexusNetTransport {
    net_client: NetClient,
    udp_socket_handle: u32, // Re-use a single UDP socket for all fetches
}

impl NexusNetTransport {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, NetError> {
        let mut net_client = NetClient::new();
        let udp_socket_handle = net_client.open_udp_socket(0)?; // Open an ephemeral UDP socket
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Opened UDP socket with handle: {}", udp_socket_handle));
        Ok(NexusNetTransport {
            net_client,
            udp_socket_handle,
        })
    }
}

impl SwarmTransport for NexusNetTransport {
    fn fetch_chunk_from_peer(&self, peer: &PeerInfo, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Fetching chunk {} from peer {}:{}",
            alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes()), peer.ip_address[0], peer.port));

        // Serialize CID for sending
        let request_payload = postcard::to_allocvec(&cid).map_err(|_| SwarmError::NetworkError)?; // Change to SwarmError

        // Send CID request to the peer over UDP
        self.net_client.send_to(
            self.udp_socket_handle,
            peer.ip_address,
            peer.port,
            request_payload
        ).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to send request: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // Receive the response (chunk data)
        // This will block until a response is received or a timeout occurs
        // In a real system, we'd have a more robust async receive with timeouts
        let response_payload = self.net_client.recv(self.udp_socket_handle).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to receive response: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // In a real scenario, the response payload would be verified and parsed to extract the chunk data.
        // For this sketch, we assume the response_payload IS the chunk data.
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Received {} bytes for chunk {}", response_payload.len(), alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes())));
        Ok(response_payload)
    }
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

The previous code had a logical issue where `dht`, `trust_store`, and `local_aid` were moved into `GlobalSearchService` preventing their reuse for `SwarmEngine`. This update clones these values for proper initialization of both services.


"""

#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;
use core::panic::PanicInfo;

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::registry_service::RegistryService;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmEngine, SwarmTransport};
use crate::arp_dht::{InMemoryDht, PeerInfo, NodeId};
use crate::trust::{TrustStore, Aid};

// Import NexusNetTransport
use crate::swarm_engine::nexus_net_transport::NexusNetTransport;
use crate::swarm_engine::global_search::GlobalSearchService; // Import GlobalSearchService

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes (redefined for registry to avoid module conflict)
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error */ }

}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {

"""**Reasoning**:
To implement the DHT Keyspace and NodeID routing within the `registry-service` V-Node, I need to update the `RegistryService::handle_request` method in `src/registry_service.rs` to process `FindNode` and `FindValue` requests using the `SwarmEngine`'s DHT capabilities. This will allow the registry to act as a DHT participant.


"""

print("Updating vnode/registry/src/registry_service.rs to handle DHT lookup IPC messages and Global Search.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/registry_service.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::string::String;
use alloc::format;

use crate::arp_dht::{Dht, DhtValue, PeerInfo, NodeId, InMemoryDht};
use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmEngine, SwarmTransport, SwarmError};
use crate::registry_ipc::{RegistryRequest, RegistryResponse};
use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::trust::{Aid, TrustStore, TrustScore};
use crate::swarm_engine::global_search::GlobalSearchService; // Import GlobalSearchService

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error */ }
    }
}

pub struct RegistryService<T: SwarmTransport, D: Dht> {
    swarm: SwarmEngine<T, D>,
    global_search_service: GlobalSearchService<D>, // Add GlobalSearchService instance
    // In a real system, a local storage for manifests and chunks would be here
}

impl<T: SwarmTransport, D: Dht> RegistryService<T, D> {
    pub fn new(swarm: SwarmEngine<T, D>, global_search_service: GlobalSearchService<D>) -> Self {
        Self { swarm, global_search_service }
    }

    pub fn handle_request(&mut self, req: RegistryRequest) -> RegistryResponse {
        match req {
            RegistryRequest::ResolveByName { name, version } => {
                // For v0.1, we don't have a name index in DHT, so this is a stub.
                log(&alloc::format!("Registry: ResolveByName not fully implemented for '{}:{:?}'", name, version));
                RegistryResponse::Error(String::from("Name resolution not implemented"))
            }
            RegistryRequest::ResolveByCid { root } => {
                match self.swarm.get_manifest(root) {
                    Ok(m) => RegistryResponse::Manifest(m),
                    Err(SwarmError::NotFound) => RegistryResponse::NotFound,
                    Err(e) => RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to get manifest: {:?}", e)),
                }
            }
            RegistryRequest::FetchManifest { root } => {
                match self.swarm.get_manifest(root) {
                    Ok(m) => RegistryResponse::ManifestData(m),
                    Err(SwarmError::NotFound) => RegistryResponse::NotFound,
                    Err(e) => RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to fetch manifest: {:?}", e)),
                }
            }
            RegistryRequest::FetchPackage { root } => {
                match self.swarm.get_manifest(root) {
                    Ok(m) => match self.swarm.fetch_package(&m) {
                        Ok(bytes) => RegistryResponse::Package(bytes),
                        Err(e) => RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to fetch package: {:?}", e)),
                    },
                    Err(SwarmError::NotFound) => RegistryResponse::NotFound,
                    Err(e) => RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to get manifest for package: {:?}", e)),
                }
            }
            RegistryRequest::FetchChunk { cid } => {
                match self.swarm.fetch_chunk(cid) {
                    Ok(data) => RegistryResponse::ChunkData(data),
                    Err(SwarmError::NotFound) => RegistryResponse::NotFound,
                    Err(e) => RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to fetch chunk: {:?}", e)),
                }
            }
            RegistryRequest::FetchPackageManifest { root_cid } => {
                match self.swarm.get_manifest(root_cid) {
                    Ok(m) => RegistryResponse::ManifestData(m),
                    Err(SwarmError::NotFound) => RegistryResponse::NotFound,
                    Err(e) => RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to fetch package manifest: {:?}", e)),
                }
            }
            RegistryRequest::FindNode { target, limit } => {
                let peers = self.swarm.dht.find_peers(&target, limit);
                RegistryResponse::FoundPeers(peers)
            }
            RegistryRequest::FindValue { key } => {
                match self.swarm.dht.find_value(&key) {
                    Some(DhtValue::Manifest(m)) => postcard::to_allocvec(&m)
                        .map(|v| RegistryResponse::FoundValue(v))
                        .unwrap_or_else(|e| RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to serialize manifest: {:?}", e))),
                    Some(DhtValue::Peer(p)) => postcard::to_allocvec(&p)
                        .map(|v| RegistryResponse::FoundValue(v))
                        .unwrap_or_else(|e| RegistryResponse::Error(alloc::format!("Failed to serialize peer: {:?}", e))),
                    _ => RegistryResponse::NotFound,
                }
            }
            RegistryRequest::GlobalSearch(search_req) => {
                let search_resp = self.global_search_service.handle_search_request(search_req);
                RegistryResponse::Search(search_resp)
            }
        }
    }

    pub fn run_loop(&mut self, chan: &mut VNodeChannel) -> ! {
        log("RegistryService: Running main loop.");
        loop {
            match chan.recv_blocking() {
                Ok(req_data) => {
                    match postcard::from_bytes::<RegistryRequest>(&req_data) {
                        Ok(request) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("RegistryService: Received request: {:?}", request));
                            let response = self.handle_request(request);
                            let resp_data = postcard::to_allocvec(&response)
                                .unwrap_or_else(|e| {
                                    log(&alloc::format!("RegistryService: Failed to serialize response: {:?}", e));
                                    Vec::new() // Return empty vec on serialization error
                                });
                            chan.send_raw(&resp_data).unwrap_or_else(|e| log(&alloc::format!("RegistryService: Failed to send response: {:?}", e)));
                        },
                        Err(e) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("RegistryService: Failed to deserialize request: {:?}", e));
                        }
                    }
                },
                Err(e) => {
                    log(&alloc::format!("RegistryService: Error receiving IPC message: {:?}", e));
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
'''

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the `src/registry_service.rs` is updated to handle DHT lookup IPC messages, I need to update `src/swarm_engine.rs` to use `dht.find_peers` and `SwarmTransport::fetch_chunk_from_peer` which takes a `PeerInfo` argument. This ensures that `SwarmEngine` can perform reputation-weighted peer selection for fetching chunks.


"""

print("Updating src/swarm_engine.rs to utilize DHT lookup mechanisms.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/swarm_engine.rs

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::manifest::PackageManifest;
use crate::arp_dht::{Dht, DhtValue, PeerInfo, NodeId};
use crate::trust::{Aid, TrustStore};

#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum SwarmError {
    NotFound,
    NetworkError,
    InvalidChunk,
    NoAvailablePeers,
    IpcError,
}

pub trait SwarmTransport {
    // fetch_chunk_from_peer now takes PeerInfo
    fn fetch_chunk_from_peer(&self, peer: &PeerInfo, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError>;
}

pub struct SwarmEngine<T: SwarmTransport, D: Dht> {
    pub transport: T,
    pub dht: D,
    pub cache: BTreeMap<Cid, Vec<u8>>,
    pub trust_store: TrustStore, // Added TrustStore to SwarmEngine
    pub local_aid: Aid, // Local Aether Identity for peer reputation
}

impl<T: SwarmTransport, D: Dht> SwarmEngine<T, D> {
    pub fn new(transport: T, dht: D, trust_store: TrustStore, local_aid: Aid) -> Self {
        Self {
            transport,
            dht,
            cache: BTreeMap::new(),
            trust_store,
            local_aid,
        }
    }

    pub fn get_manifest(&mut self, root_cid: Cid) -> Result<PackageManifest, SwarmError> {
        // First try to find in local cache
        if let Some(DhtValue::Manifest(m)) = self.dht.find_value(&root_cid) {
            return Ok(m);
        }

        // If not in local cache, query DHT for peers who have this manifest
        let target_node_id = NodeId(root_cid.0); // Use CID as target for NodeId lookup
        let peers = self.dht.find_peers(&target_node_id, 8);

        if peers.is_empty() {
            return Err(SwarmError::NoAvailablePeers);
        }

        // Sort peers by reputation (higher score first)
        let mut sorted_peers = peers;
        sorted_peers.sort_by(|a, b| {
            let score_a = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&a.aid).score;
            let score_b = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&b.aid).score;
            score_b.partial_cmp(&score_a).unwrap_or(core::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
        });

        for peer in sorted_peers {
            // In a real system, this would be an IPC call to the peer for the manifest
            // For this in-memory DHT, we simulate finding the manifest value from the DHT itself.
            // A real SwarmTransport would have a method like fetch_manifest_from_peer.
            if let Some(DhtValue::Manifest(m)) = self.dht.find_value(&root_cid) { // Simplified for in-memory DHT
                // Store the manifest in local DHT cache
                self.dht.store(root_cid, DhtValue::Manifest(m.clone()));
                return Ok(m);
            }
        }

        Err(SwarmError::NotFound)
    }

    pub fn fetch_chunk(&mut self, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        // First try to find in local cache
        if let Some(data) = self.cache.get(&cid) {
            return Ok(data.clone());
        }

        // Query DHT for peers who have this chunk
        let target_node_id = NodeId(cid.0); // Use CID as target for NodeId lookup
        let peers = self.dht.find_peers(&target_node_id, 8);

        if peers.is_empty() {
            return Err(SwarmError::NoAvailablePeers);
        }

        // Sort peers by reputation (higher score first)
        let mut sorted_peers = peers;
        sorted_peers.sort_by(|a, b| {
            let score_a = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&a.aid).score;
            let score_b = self.trust_store.get_peer_trust(&b.aid).score;
            score_b.partial_cmp(&score_a).unwrap_or(core::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
        });

        for peer in sorted_peers {
            // Use the transport to fetch the chunk from the peer
            match self.transport.fetch_chunk_from_peer(&peer, cid) {
                Ok(data) => {
                    // Verify CID hash after fetching
                    if Cid::hash(&data) != cid {
                        log(&alloc::format!("SwarmEngine: Received invalid chunk from peer {:?} for CID {:?}", peer.id, cid));
                        // In a real system, update peer's reputation negatively
                        return Err(SwarmError::InvalidChunk);
                    }
                    self.cache.insert(cid, data.clone());
                    return Ok(data);
                },
                Err(e) => {
                    log(&alloc::format!("SwarmEngine: Failed to fetch chunk from peer {:?}: {:?}", peer.id, e));
                    // In a real system, update peer's reputation negatively
                    continue; // Try next peer
                }
            }
        }

        Err(SwarmError::NotFound)
    }

    pub fn fetch_package(&mut self, manifest: &PackageManifest) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(manifest.total_size() as usize);
        for chunk_entry in &manifest.chunks {
            let data = self.fetch_chunk(chunk_entry.cid)?; // Use the new fetch_chunk
            out.extend_from_slice(&data);
        }
        Ok(out)
    }

    // Temporary log function, should be replaced by proper V-Node logging
    fn log(msg: &str) {
        // In a real V-Node, this would use a syscall for logging
        // For now, let's just use `println!` for simulation if `std` is available,
        // or a dummy function if it's strictly `no_std`.
        // Assuming a simple `log` function is available in the V-Node context for now.
    }
}

'''

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the IPC messages for DHT lookups and the `SwarmEngine` are updated, I will define the `NexusNetTransport` struct and implement the `SwarmTransport` trait. This will allow `NexusNetTransport` to use `libnexus-net`'s `send_to` and `recv` methods for fetching chunks over UDP, completing the implementation of a real network transport.


"""

write("vnode/registry/src/main.rs", rust_code)

print("Creating src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs to implement SwarmTransport.")

rust_code = r'''
// src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs

#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::format;

use crate::cid::Cid;
use crate::swarm_engine::{SwarmTransport, SwarmError};
use crate::arp_dht::PeerInfo;
use libnexus_net::{NetClient, NetError};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = crate::syscall::syscall3(
            crate::syscall::SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != crate::syscall::SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

pub struct NexusNetTransport {
    net_client: NetClient,
    udp_socket_handle: u32, // Re-use a single UDP socket for all fetches
}

impl NexusNetTransport {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, NetError> {
        let mut net_client = NetClient::new();
        let udp_socket_handle = net_client.open_udp_socket(0)?; // Open an ephemeral UDP socket
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Opened UDP socket with handle: {}", udp_socket_handle));
        Ok(NexusNetTransport {
            net_client,
            udp_socket_handle,
        })
    }
}

impl SwarmTransport for NexusNetTransport {
    fn fetch_chunk_from_peer(&self, peer: &PeerInfo, cid: Cid) -> Result<Vec<u8>, SwarmError> {
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Fetching chunk {} from peer {}:{}",
            alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes()), peer.ip_address[0], peer.port));

        // Serialize CID for sending
        let request_payload = postcard::to_allocvec(&cid).map_err(|_| SwarmError::NetworkError)?;

        // Send CID request to the peer over UDP
        self.net_client.send_to(
            self.udp_socket_handle,
            peer.ip_address,
            peer.port,
            request_payload
        ).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to send request: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // Receive the response (chunk data)
        // This will block until a response is received or a timeout occurs
        // In a real system, we'd have a more robust async receive with timeouts
        let response_payload = self.net_client.recv(self.udp_socket_handle).map_err(|e| {
            log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Failed to receive response: {:?}", e));
            SwarmError::NetworkError
        })?;

        // In a real scenario, the response payload would be verified and parsed to extract the chunk data.
        // For this sketch, we assume the response_payload IS the chunk data.
        log(&alloc::format!("NexusNetTransport: Received {} bytes for chunk {}", response_payload.len(), alloc::format!("{:?}", cid.as_bytes())));
        Ok(response_payload)
    }
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

write("src/swarm_engine/nexus_net_transport.rs", rust_code)

Updating `kernel/syscall.rs` with `SYS_IPC_RECV_NONBLOCKING` and the simulated ICMP Echo Request packet.

```rust
// For simplicity, assume these modules/functions exist or are stubbed in the kernel context
// In a real kernel, these would be properly defined and imported.

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::string::String;
use spin::Mutex;
use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};

mod console { // Temporary stub for console output
    // In a real kernel, these would write to serial port or framebuffer
    pub fn print(s: &str) { /* ... */ }
    pub fn print_u64(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
    pub fn print_hex(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
}

mod task { // Temporary stub for task management
    pub struct Task;
    impl Task {
        pub fn has_cap(&self, cap: crate::caps::Capability) -> bool {
            // For stub, assume all capabilities are granted if not explicitly checked for testing
            match cap {
                crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess => true, // Allow net access for net-bridge development
                crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite => true,
                crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead => true,
                _ => false // Deny others by default
            }
        }
    }
    pub fn get_current_task() -> Task { Task } // Stub to return a dummy task
    pub fn block_current_on_channel(_id: u32) { /* ... */ }
}

mod ipc { // Temporary stub for IPC
    extern crate alloc;
    use alloc::collections::VecDeque;
    use alloc::vec::Vec;

    pub type ChannelId = u32;

    pub struct Message { pub data: Vec<u8> }
    pub struct KernelChannel { queue: VecDeque<Message> }

    static CHANNELS: Mutex<[Option<KernelChannel>; 32]> = Mutex::new([None; 32]);

    pub fn kernel_send(id: ChannelId, data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), &'static str> {
        let mut chans = CHANNELS.lock();
        if id as usize >= chans.len() {
            return Err("Channel ID out of bounds");
        }
        if chans[id as usize].is_none() {
            chans[id as usize] = Some(KernelChannel { queue: VecDeque::new() });
        }
        if let Some(chan) = chans[id as usize].as_mut() {
            chan.queue.push_back(Message { data: data.to_vec() });
            // In a real kernel, this would also wake up blocked tasks
            Ok(())
        } else {
            Err("Channel not found (should not happen after insertion)")
        }
    }

    pub fn kernel_recv(id: ChannelId) -> Option<alloc::vec::Vec<u8>> {
        let mut chans = CHANNELS.lock();
        if id as usize >= chans.len() {
            return None;
        }
        chans[id as usize].as_mut()?.queue.pop_front().map(|m| m.data)
    }
}

mod timer { // Temporary stub for timer
    pub static mut TICKS: u64 = 0;
}

mod caps { // Temporary stub for capabilities
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub enum Capability { LogWrite, TimeRead, NetworkAccess, StorageAccess }
}

mod arch { // Temporary stub for arch-specific stuff
    pub mod irq {
        pub fn register_irq_handler(_irq: u8, _chan: u32) { /* ... */ }
        pub fn acknowledge_irq(_irq: u8) { /* ... */ }
    }
    pub mod dma { // Stub for DMA management
        extern crate alloc;
        use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
        use spin::Mutex;
        use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};

        static NEXT_HANDLE: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(1);
        static DMA_BUFFERS: Mutex<BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u8>>> = Mutex::new(BTreeMap::new());

        pub fn alloc_dma_buffer(size: usize) -> Option<u64> {
            let handle = NEXT_HANDLE.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            let buffer = Vec::with_capacity(size);
            buffers.insert(handle, buffer);
            Some(handle)
        }

        pub fn free_dma_buffer(handle: u64) {
            let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            buffers.remove(&handle);
        }

        pub fn get_dma_buffer_ptr(handle: u64) -> Option<*mut u8> {
            let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            buffers.get_mut(&handle).map(|buf| buf.as_mut_ptr())
        }

        pub fn get_dma_buffer_capacity(handle: u64) -> Option<usize> {
            let buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            buffers.get(&handle).map(|buf| buf.capacity())
        }

        pub fn set_dma_buffer_len(handle: u64, len: usize) -> Result<(), &'static str> {
            let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            if let Some(buf) = buffers.get_mut(&handle) {
                if len <= buf.capacity() {
                    unsafe { buf.set_len(len); }
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err("Length exceeds capacity")
                }
            } else {
                Err("DMA handle not found")
            }
        }

    }
}

// kernel/syscall.rs

pub const E_ACC_DENIED: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE;
pub const E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF;
pub const E_ERROR: u64 = 1;
pub const SUCCESS: u64 = 0;

pub const SYS_LOG: u64 = 0;
pub const SYS_IPC_SEND: u64 = 1;
pub const SYS_IPC_RECV: u64 = 2;
pub const SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN: u64 = 3;
pub const SYS_TIME: u64 = 4;
pub const SYS_IRQ_REGISTER: u64 = 5;
pub const SYS_NET_RX_POLL: u64 = 6;
pub const SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF: u64 = 7;
pub const SYS_NET_FREE_BUF: u64 = 8;
pub const SYS_NET_TX: u64 = 9;
pub const SYS_IRQ_ACK: u64 = 10;
pub const SYS_GET_DMA_BUF_PTR: u64 = 11;
pub const SYS_SET_DMA_BUF_LEN: u64 = 12;
pub const SYS_IPC_RECV_NONBLOCKING: u64 = 13; // New syscall for non-blocking receive

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn syscall_dispatch(n: u64, a1: u64, a2: u64, a3: u64) -> u64 {
    let current_task = crate::task::get_current_task();

    match n {
        SYS_LOG => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            let ptr = a1 as *const u8;
            let len = a2 as usize;
            let msg = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(ptr, len) };
            if let Ok(s) = core::str::from_utf8(msg) {
                crate::console::print("[V-Node Log] ");
                crate::console::print(s);
                crate::console::print("\n");
                SUCCESS
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_SEND => {
            let buf = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(a2 as *const u8, a3 as usize) };
            if crate::ipc::kernel_send(a1 as u32, buf).is_ok() { SUCCESS } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_RECV => {
            let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
            let out_cap = a3 as usize;
            if let Some(data) = crate::ipc::kernel_recv(a1 as u32) {
                if data.len() <= out_cap {
                    unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(data.as_ptr(), out_ptr, data.len()); }
                    data.len() as u64
                } else {
                    E_ERROR
                }
            } else { SUCCESS } // No data or empty message, return 0 len
        }
        SYS_IPC_RECV_NONBLOCKING => {
            let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
            let out_cap = a3 as usize;
            if let Some(data) = crate::ipc::kernel_recv(a1 as u32) {
                if data.len() <= out_cap {
                    unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(data.as_ptr(), out_ptr, data.len()); }
                    data.len() as u64
                } else {
                    E_ERROR
                }
            } else { SUCCESS } // Return SUCCESS (0) if no data available, indicating non-blocking no-op
        }
        SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN => {
            crate::task::block_current_on_channel(a1 as u32);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_TIME => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            unsafe { crate::timer::TICKS }
        }
        SYS_IRQ_REGISTER => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::irq::register_irq_handler(a1 as u8, a2 as u32);
            crate::console::print("[Kernel] Registering IRQ ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a1);
            crate::console::print(" for channel ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a2);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_NET_RX_POLL => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            let dma_handle = a2;
            let out_cap = a3 as usize;

            // Simulated ICMP Echo Request packet targeting 10.0.2.15
            // Destination MAC: 02:00:00:00:00:01 (AetherNet's MAC)
            // Source MAC: 00:00:00:00:00:02 (Simulated sender)
            // EtherType: IPv4 (0x0800)
            // IP Header:
            //   Version: 4, IHL: 5 (20 bytes)
            //   Total Length: 84 (20 IP + 8 ICMP + 56 data)
            //   TTL: 64
            //   Protocol: ICMP (1)
            //   Source IP: 10.0.2.1 (Simulated sender)
            //   Destination IP: 10.0.2.15 (AetherNet Service)
            // ICMP Header:
            //   Type: Echo Request (8)
            //   Code: 0
            //   Checksum: calculated later
            //   ID: 1, Sequence: 1
            // Data: 56 bytes of 'A'
            let simulated_packet: [u8; 98] = [
                // Ethernet Header (14 bytes)
                0x02, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01, // Destination MAC (AetherNet's MAC)
                0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x02, // Source MAC (Simulated Sender)
                0x08, 0x00,                         // EtherType: IPv4
                // IPv4 Header (20 bytes)
                0x45, 0x00,                         // Version (4) + IHL (5), DSCP (0)
                0x00, 0x54,                         // Total Length: 84 bytes (20 IP + 8 ICMP + 56 Data)
                0x00, 0x01, 0x00, 0x00,             // Identification, Flags, Fragment Offset
                0x40, 0x01,                         // TTL (64), Protocol (ICMP)
                0x7C, 0x0A,                         // Header Checksum (placeholder, will be calculated by smoltcp)
                0x0A, 0x00, 0x02, 0x01,             // Source IP: 10.0.2.1
                0x0A, 0x00, 0x02, 0x0F,             // Destination IP: 10.0.2.15
                // ICMP Echo Request (8 bytes + 56 bytes data = 64 bytes total for ICMP payload)
                0x08, 0x00,                         // Type (8: Echo Request), Code (0)
                0xF7, 0xFF,                         // Checksum (placeholder, will be calculated by smoltcp)
                0x00, 0x01,                         // ID (1)
                0x00, 0x01,                         // Sequence (1)
                // ICMP Data (56 bytes - 'A' * 56)
                0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41,
                0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41,
                0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41,
                0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41,
                0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41,
                0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41,
                0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41,
            ];
            let packet_len = simulated_packet.len();

            if packet_len <= out_cap {
                if let Some(buf_ptr) = crate::arch::dma::get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
                    unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(simulated_packet.as_ptr(), buf_ptr, packet_len); }
                    if crate::arch::dma::set_dma_buffer_len(dma_handle, packet_len).is_ok() {
                        packet_len as u64
                    } else {
                        E_ERROR
                    }
                } else {
                    E_ERROR
                }
            } else {
                E_ERROR
            }
        }
        SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ALLOC
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            if let Some(handle) = crate::arch::dma::alloc_dma_buffer(a1 as usize) {
                handle
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_NET_FREE_BUF => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ALLOC
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::dma::free_dma_buffer(a1);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_NET_TX => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::console::print("[Kernel] Queuing packet for TX, handle: ");
            crate::console::print_hex(a2);
            crate::console::print(", len: ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a3);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_IRQ_ACK => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or specific IRQ cap
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::irq::acknowledge_irq(a1 as u8);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_GET_DMA_BUF_PTR => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ACCESS
                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            if let Some(ptr) = crate::arch::dma::get_dma_buffer_ptr(a1) {
                ptr as u64
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_SET_DMA_BUF_LEN => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ACCESS
                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            if crate::arch::dma::set_dma_buffer_len(a1, a2 as usize).is_ok() {
                SUCCESS
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        _ => E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL,
    }
}
```

## Update kernel/syscall.rs

### Subtask:
Provide the complete and latest Rust code for kernel/syscall.rs, ensuring it includes SYS_IPC_RECV_NONBLOCKING and the simulated ICMP Echo Request packet logic for SYS_NET_RX_POLL.

**Reasoning**:
The subtask requires writing the complete Rust code for `kernel/syscall.rs` to the specified file path. I will use the `%%writefile` magic command for this.
"""

# Commented out IPython magic to ensure Python compatibility.
# %%writefile /content/drive/MyDrive/AetherOS/current/aetheros/kernel/src/syscall.rs
# // For simplicity, assume these modules/functions exist or are stubbed in the kernel context
# // In a real kernel, these would be properly defined and imported.
# 
# extern crate alloc;
# use alloc::vec::Vec;
# use alloc::string::String;
# use spin::Mutex;
# use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
# use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
# 
# mod console { // Temporary stub for console output
#     // In a real kernel, these would write to serial port or framebuffer
#     pub fn print(s: &str) { /* ... */ }
#     pub fn print_u64(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
#     pub fn print_hex(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
# }
# 
# mod task { // Temporary stub for task management
#     pub struct Task;
#     impl Task {
#         pub fn has_cap(&self, cap: crate::caps::Capability) -> bool {
#             // For stub, assume all capabilities are granted if not explicitly checked for testing
#             match cap {
#                 crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess => true, // Allow net access for net-bridge development
#                 crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite => true,
#                 crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead => true,
#                 _ => false // Deny others by default
#             }
#         }
#     }
#     pub fn get_current_task() -> Task { Task } // Stub to return a dummy task
#     pub fn block_current_on_channel(_id: u32) { /* ... */ }
# }
# 
# mod ipc { // Temporary stub for IPC
#     extern crate alloc;
#     use alloc::collections::VecDeque;
#     use alloc::vec::Vec;
# 
#     pub type ChannelId = u32;
# 
#     pub struct Message { pub data: Vec<u8> }
#     pub struct KernelChannel { queue: VecDeque<Message> }
# 
#     static CHANNELS: Mutex<[Option<KernelChannel>; 32]> = Mutex::new([None; 32]);
# 
#     pub fn kernel_send(id: ChannelId, data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), &'static str> {
#         let mut chans = CHANNELS.lock();
#         if let Some(chan) = chans[id as usize].as_mut() {
#             chan.queue.push_back(Message { data: data.to_vec() });
#             // In a real kernel, this would also wake up blocked tasks
#             Ok(())
#         } else {
#             Err("Channel not found")
#         }
#     }
# 
#     pub fn kernel_recv(id: ChannelId) -> Option<alloc::vec::Vec<u8>> {
#         let mut chans = CHANNELS.lock();
#         chans[id as usize].as_mut()?.queue.pop_front().map(|m| m.data)
#     }
# }
# 
# mod timer { // Temporary stub for timer
#     pub static mut TICKS: u64 = 0;
# }
# 
# mod caps { // Temporary stub for capabilities
#     #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
#     pub enum Capability { LogWrite, TimeRead, NetworkAccess, StorageAccess }
# }
# 
# mod arch { // Temporary stub for arch-specific stuff
#     pub mod irq {
#         pub fn register_irq_handler(_irq: u8, _chan: u32) { /* ... */ }
#         pub fn acknowledge_irq(_irq: u8) { /* ... */ }
#     }
#     pub mod dma { // Stub for DMA management
#         extern crate alloc;
#         use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
#         use spin::Mutex;
#         use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
# 
#         static NEXT_HANDLE: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(1);
#         static DMA_BUFFERS: Mutex<BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u8>>> = Mutex::new(BTreeMap::new());
# 
#         pub fn alloc_dma_buffer(size: usize) -> Option<u64> {
#             let handle = NEXT_HANDLE.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
#             let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
#             // Allocate a Vec as a placeholder for a DMA page. Size of Vec represents allocated capacity.
#             let buffer = Vec::with_capacity(size);
#             buffers.insert(handle, buffer);
#             Some(handle)
#         }
# 
#         pub fn free_dma_buffer(handle: u64) {
#             let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
#             buffers.remove(&handle);
#         }
# 
#         pub fn get_dma_buffer_ptr(handle: u64) -> Option<*mut u8> {
#             let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
#             buffers.get_mut(&handle).map(|buf| buf.as_mut_ptr())
#         }
# 
#         pub fn get_dma_buffer_capacity(handle: u64) -> Option<usize> {
#             let buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
#             buffers.get(&handle).map(|buf| buf.capacity())
#         }
# 
#         pub fn set_dma_buffer_len(handle: u64, len: usize) -> Result<(), &'static str> {
#             let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
#             if let Some(buf) = buffers.get_mut(&handle) {
#                 if len <= buf.capacity() {
#                     unsafe { buf.set_len(len); }
#                     Ok(())
#                 } else {
#                     Err("Length exceeds capacity")
#                 }
#             } else {
#                 Err("DMA handle not found")
#             }
#         }
# 
#     }
# }
# 
# // kernel/syscall.rs
# 
# pub const E_ACC_DENIED: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE;
# pub const E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF;
# pub const E_ERROR: u64 = 1;
# pub const SUCCESS: u64 = 0;
# 
# pub const SYS_LOG: u64 = 0;
# pub const SYS_IPC_SEND: u64 = 1;
# pub const SYS_IPC_RECV: u64 = 2;
# pub const SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN: u64 = 3;
# pub const SYS_TIME: u64 = 4;
# pub const SYS_IRQ_REGISTER: u64 = 5;
# pub const SYS_NET_RX_POLL: u64 = 6;
# pub const SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF: u64 = 7;
# pub const SYS_NET_FREE_BUF: u64 = 8;
# pub const SYS_NET_TX: u64 = 9;
# pub const SYS_IRQ_ACK: u64 = 10;
# pub const SYS_GET_DMA_BUF_PTR: u64 = 11;
# pub const SYS_SET_DMA_BUF_LEN: u64 = 12;
# pub const SYS_IPC_RECV_NONBLOCKING: u64 = 13; // New syscall for non-blocking receive
# 
# #[no_mangle]
# pub extern "C" fn syscall_dispatch(n: u64, a1: u64, a2: u64, a3: u64) -> u64 {
#     let current_task = crate::task::get_current_task();
# 
#     match n {
#         SYS_LOG => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
#             }
#             let ptr = a1 as *const u8;
#             let len = a2 as usize;
#             let msg = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(ptr, len) };
#             if let Ok(s) = core::str::from_utf8(msg) {
#                 crate::console::print("[V-Node Log] ");
#                 crate::console::print(s);
#                 crate::console::print("\n");
#                 SUCCESS
#             } else { E_ERROR }
#         }
#         SYS_IPC_SEND => {
#             let buf = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(a2 as *const u8, a3 as usize) };
#             if crate::ipc::kernel_send(a1 as u32, buf).is_ok() { SUCCESS } else { E_ERROR }
#         }
#         SYS_IPC_RECV => {
#             let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
#             let out_cap = a3 as usize;
#             if let Some(data) = crate::ipc::kernel_recv(a1 as u32) {
#                 if data.len() <= out_cap {
#                     unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(data.as_ptr(), out_ptr, data.len()); }
#                     data.len() as u64
#                 } else {
#                     E_ERROR
#                 }
#             } else { SUCCESS } // No data or empty message, return 0 len
#         }
#         SYS_IPC_RECV_NONBLOCKING => {
#             let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
#             let out_cap = a3 as usize;
#             if let Some(data) = crate::ipc::kernel_recv(a1 as u32) {
#                 if data.len() <= out_cap {
#                     unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(data.as_ptr(), out_ptr, data.len()); }
#                     data.len() as u64
#                 } else {
#                     E_ERROR
#                 }
#             } else { SUCCESS } // Return SUCCESS (0) if no data available, indicating non-blocking no-op
#         }
#         SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN => {
#             crate::task::block_current_on_channel(a1 as u32);
#             SUCCESS
#         }
#         SYS_TIME => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
#             }
#             unsafe { crate::timer::TICKS }
#         }
#         SYS_IRQ_REGISTER => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
#             }
#             crate::arch::irq::register_irq_handler(a1 as u8, a2 as u32);
#             crate::console::print("[Kernel] Registering IRQ ");
#             crate::console::print_u64(a1);
#             crate::console::print(" for channel ");
#             crate::console::print_u64(a2);
#             crate::console::print("\n");
#             SUCCESS
#         }
#         SYS_NET_RX_POLL => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
#             }
#             let dma_handle = a2;
#             let out_cap = a3 as usize;
# 
#             let simulated_packet: [u8; 42] = [
#                 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF,
#                 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01,
#                 0x08, 0x06,
#                 0x00, 0x01, 0x08, 0x00, 0x06, 0x04,
#                 0x00, 0x01,
#                 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01,
#                 0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x01,
#                 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00,
#                 0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x02,
#             ];
#             let packet_len = simulated_packet.len();
# 
#             if packet_len <= out_cap {
#                 if let Some(buf_ptr) = crate::arch::dma::get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
#                     unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(simulated_packet.as_ptr(), buf_ptr, packet_len); }
#                     if crate::arch::dma::set_dma_buffer_len(dma_handle, packet_len).is_ok() {
#                         packet_len as u64
#                     } else {
#                         E_ERROR
#                     }
#                 } else {
#                     E_ERROR
#                 }
#             } else {
#                 E_ERROR
#             }
#         }
#         SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
#             }
#             if let Some(handle) = crate::arch::dma::alloc_dma_buffer(a1 as usize) {
#                 handle
#             } else { E_ERROR }

#         SYS_NET_FREE_BUF => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;

#             crate::arch::dma::free_dma_buffer(a1);

#         }
#         SYS_NET_TX => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;

#             crate::console::print("[Kernel] Queuing packet for TX, handle: ");
#             crate::console::print_hex(a2);
#             crate::console::print(", len: ");
#             crate::console::print_u64(a3);
#             crate::console::print("\n");
#             SUCCESS
#         }
#         SYS_IRQ_ACK => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
#                 return E_ACC_DENIED;
#             }
#             crate::arch::irq::acknowledge_irq(a1 as u8);
#             SUCCESS
#         }
#         SYS_GET_DMA_BUF_PTR => {
#             if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {

"""## Update vnode/net-stack/aethernet_device.rs

### Subtask:
Provide the complete and latest Rust code for `vnode/net-stack/aethernet_device.rs`, ensuring it properly implements `smoltcp::phy::Device` and uses the correct IPC mechanism for TX and RX packets with the `net-bridge` V-Node.

**Reasoning**:
I need to provide the complete and latest Rust code for `vnode/net-stack/aethernet_device.rs`. This involves defining the required structs and traits for `smoltcp` integration, implementing the IPC mechanism for packet transfer with `net-bridge`, and including necessary syscall helper functions.
"""

print("Creating vnode/net-stack/aethernet_device.rs for smoltcp device implementation.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use alloc::collections::VecDeque;
use smoltcp::phy::{Device, RxToken, TxToken, Checksum, DeviceCapabilities};
use smoltcp::time::Instant;
use smoltcp::wire::{EthernetAddress, HardwareAddress};

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall3, SYS_LOG, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, SYS_GET_DMA_BUF_PTR, syscall2, E_ACC_DENIED, SYS_SET_DMA_BUF_LEN};
use crate::ipc::net_ipc::NetPacketMsg;

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for allocating a DMA buffer
pub fn net_alloc_buf(size: usize) -> Result<u64, u64> {
    unsafe {
        let handle = syscall2(SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, size as u64, 0);
        if handle == E_ERROR || handle == E_ACC_DENIED { Err(handle) } else { Ok(handle) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for freeing a DMA buffer
pub fn net_free_buf(handle: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, handle, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for transmitting a network packet (used by PacketTxToken consume)
// This is publicly exposed for other internal modules if needed, but the IPC is primary.
pub fn net_tx(_iface_id: u64, _buf_handle: u64, _len: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    // This function is here to satisfy the instructions, but its direct use
    // for TX is largely replaced by IPC with net-bridge.
    // In a pure IPC model, this would not be called directly for TX.
    Ok( crate::syscall::SUCCESS )
}

// Temporary syscall to get pointer to DMA buffer
pub fn get_dma_buffer_ptr(handle: u64) -> Result<*mut u8, u64> {
    unsafe {
        let ptr = syscall2(SYS_GET_DMA_BUF_PTR, handle, 0);
        if ptr == E_ERROR || ptr == E_ACC_DENIED { Err(ptr) } else { Ok(ptr as *mut u8) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall to set DMA buffer length
pub fn set_dma_buffer_len(handle: u64, len: usize) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_SET_DMA_BUF_LEN, handle, len as u64);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

/// Represents a single received packet buffer for smoltcp.
pub struct PacketRxToken<'a> {
    buffer: &'a mut [u8],
    dma_handle: u64,
}

impl<'a> RxToken for PacketRxToken<'a> {
    fn consume<R, F>(self, _timestamp: Instant, f: F) -> R
    where
        F: FnOnce(&mut [u8]) -> R,
    {
        // The smoltcp stack consumes the packet data
        let result = f(self.buffer);
        // After consumption, free the DMA buffer
        if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(self.dma_handle) {
            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free RX DMA buffer: {}", e));
        }
        result
    }
}

/// Represents a single transmitted packet buffer for smoltcp.
pub struct PacketTxToken<'a> {
    buffer: &'a mut [u8],
    dma_handle: u64,
    len: usize,
    iface_id: u64,
    net_bridge_chan_id: u32, // Channel ID to net-bridge V-Node
}

impl<'a> TxToken for PacketTxToken<'a> {
    fn consume<R, F>(mut self, _timestamp: Instant, f: F) -> R
    where
        F: FnOnce(&mut [u8]) -> R,
    {
        let result = f(self.buffer);
        // After smoltcp fills the buffer, send it to net-bridge for transmission
        let mut net_bridge_chan = VNodeChannel::new(self.net_bridge_chan_id);
        let msg = NetPacketMsg::TxPacket { dma_handle: self.dma_handle, len: self.len as u64 };
        net_bridge_chan.send(&msg).unwrap_or_else(|_| log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to send TxPacket to net-bridge for handle: {}", self.dma_handle)));

        // The net-bridge V-Node will free the DMA buffer after transmission (no direct net_tx call here)
        result
    }
}

/// AetherNetDevice implements smoltcp::phy::Device for communication with net-bridge V-Node.
pub struct AetherNetDevice {
    iface_id: u64, // Interface ID, typically 0 for the first NIC
    net_bridge_chan_id: u32, // Channel ID to net-bridge V-Node for TxPacket and RxPacket
    rx_packet_queue: VecDeque<(u64, u64)>, // Queue of (dma_handle, len) for received packets
}

impl AetherNetDevice {
    pub fn new(iface_id: u64, net_bridge_channel_id: u32) -> Self {
        AetherNetDevice {
            iface_id,
            net_bridge_chan_id: net_bridge_channel_id,
            rx_packet_queue: VecDeque::new(),
        }
    }

    pub fn enqueue_rx_packet(&mut self, dma_handle: u64, len: u64) {
        self.rx_packet_queue.push_back((dma_handle, len));
    }
}

impl<'a> Device<'a> for AetherNetDevice {
    type RxToken = PacketRxToken<'a>;
    type TxToken = PacketTxToken<'a>;

    fn capabilities(&self) -> DeviceCapabilities {
        let mut caps = DeviceCapabilities::default();
        caps.max_transmission_unit = 1500;
        caps.max_burst_size = Some(1);
        caps.checksum = Checksum::None; // Checksum offloading not simulated
        caps.medium = smoltcp::phy::Medium::Ethernet;
        caps
    }

    fn receive(&'a mut self, _timestamp: Instant) -> Option<(Self::RxToken, Self::TxToken)> {
        // Consume from the queue of packets pushed by net-bridge
        if let Some((dma_handle, len)) = self.rx_packet_queue.pop_front() {
            if let Ok(buf_ptr) = get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
                let buffer = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(buf_ptr, len as usize) };
                Some((PacketRxToken { buffer, dma_handle }, PacketTxToken {
                    buffer: &mut [], // dummy buffer for TxToken when not transmitting
                    dma_handle: 0,
                    len: 0,
                    iface_id: self.iface_id,
                    net_bridge_chan_id: self.net_bridge_chan_id // Pass the channel ID
                }))
            } else {
                log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to get buffer pointer for RX DMA handle {}. Freeing it.", dma_handle));
                // Free the DMA buffer if ptr is invalid
                if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(dma_handle) { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free RX DMA buffer (ptr error, queue): {}", e)); }
                None
            }
        } else {
            // No packets from net-bridge in queue
            None
        }
    }

    fn transmit(&'a mut self, _timestamp: Instant) -> Option<Self::TxToken> {
        // Allocate a DMA buffer for outgoing packet
        let dma_handle = match net_alloc_buf(1536) {
            Ok(h) => h,
            Err(e) => { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to alloc TX DMA buffer: {}", e)); return None; }
        };

        if let Ok(buf_ptr) = get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
            let buffer = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(buf_ptr, 1536) }; // Max MTU
            Some(PacketTxToken { buffer, dma_handle, len: 1536, iface_id: self.iface_id, net_bridge_chan_id: self.net_bridge_chan_id })
        } else {
            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to get buffer pointer for TX DMA handle {}. Freeing it.", dma_handle));
            if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(dma_handle) { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free TX DMA buffer (ptr error): {}", e)); } // Try to free if ptr couldn't be obtained
            None
        }
    }
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
The previous command only printed the code. Now, I need to write the `rust_code` to the specified file `vnode/net-stack/aethernet_device.rs` to complete the subtask.


"""

#         }
#         result
#     }
# }
# 
# /// Represents a single transmitted packet buffer for smoltcp.
# pub struct PacketTxToken<'a> {
#     buffer: &'a mut [u8],
#     dma_handle: u64,
#     len: usize,
#     iface_id: u64,
#     net_bridge_chan_id: u32, // Channel ID to net-bridge V-Node
# }
# 
# impl<'a> TxToken for PacketTxToken<'a> {
#     fn consume<R, F>(mut self, _timestamp: Instant, f: F) -> R
#     where
#         F: FnOnce(&mut [u8]) -> R,
#     {
#         let result = f(self.buffer);
#         // After smoltcp fills the buffer, send it to net-bridge for transmission
#         let mut net_bridge_chan = VNodeChannel::new(self.net_bridge_chan_id);
#         let msg = NetPacketMsg::TxPacket { dma_handle: self.dma_handle, len: self.len as u64 };
#         net_bridge_chan.send(&msg).unwrap_or_else(|_| log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to send TxPacket to net-bridge for handle: {}", self.dma_handle)));
# 
#         // The net-bridge V-Node will free the DMA buffer after transmission (no direct net_tx call here)
#         result
#     }
# }
# 
# /// AetherNetDevice implements smoltcp::phy::Device for communication with net-bridge V-Node.
# pub struct AetherNetDevice {
#     iface_id: u64, // Interface ID, typically 0 for the first NIC
#     net_bridge_chan_id: u32, // Channel ID to net-bridge V-Node for TxPacket and RxPacket
#     rx_packet_queue: VecDeque<(u64, u64)>, // Queue of (dma_handle, len) for received packets
# }
# 
# impl AetherNetDevice {
#     pub fn new(iface_id: u64, net_bridge_channel_id: u32) -> Self {
#         AetherNetDevice {
#             iface_id,
#             net_bridge_chan_id: net_bridge_channel_id,
#             rx_packet_queue: VecDeque::new(),
#         }
#     }
# 
#     pub fn enqueue_rx_packet(&mut self, dma_handle: u64, len: u64) {
#         self.rx_packet_queue.push_back((dma_handle, len));
#     }
# }
# 
# impl<'a> Device<'a> for AetherNetDevice {
#     type RxToken = PacketRxToken<'a>;
#     type TxToken = PacketTxToken<'a>;
# 
#     fn capabilities(&self) -> DeviceCapabilities {
#         let mut caps = DeviceCapabilities::default();
#         caps.max_transmission_unit = 1500;
#         caps.max_burst_size = Some(1);
#         caps.checksum = Checksum::None; // Checksum offloading not simulated
#         caps.medium = smoltcp::phy::Medium::Ethernet;
#         caps
#     }
# 
#     fn receive(&'a mut self, _timestamp: Instant) -> Option<(Self::RxToken, Self::TxToken)> {
#         // Consume from the queue of packets pushed by net-bridge
#         if let Some((dma_handle, len)) = self.rx_packet_queue.pop_front() {
#             if let Ok(buf_ptr) = get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
#                 let buffer = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(buf_ptr, len as usize) };
#                 Some((PacketRxToken { buffer, dma_handle }, PacketTxToken {
#                     buffer: &mut [], // dummy buffer for TxToken when not transmitting
#                     dma_handle: 0,
#                     len: 0,
#                     iface_id: self.iface_id,
#                     net_bridge_chan_id: self.net_bridge_chan_id // Pass the channel ID
#                 }))
#             } else {
#                 log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to get buffer pointer for RX DMA handle {}. Freeing it.", dma_handle));
#                 // Free the DMA buffer if ptr is invalid
#                 if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(dma_handle) { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free RX DMA buffer (ptr error, queue): {}", e)); }
#                 None
#             }
#         } else {
#             // No packets from net-bridge in queue
#             None
#         }
#     }
# 
#     fn transmit(&'a mut self, _timestamp: Instant) -> Option<Self::TxToken> {
#         // Allocate a DMA buffer for outgoing packet
#         let dma_handle = match net_alloc_buf(1536) {
#             Ok(h) => h,
#             Err(e) => { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to alloc TX DMA buffer: {}", e)); return None; }
#         };
# 
#         if let Ok(buf_ptr) = get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
#             let buffer = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(buf_ptr, 1536) }; // Max MTU
#             Some(PacketTxToken { buffer, dma_handle, len: 1536, iface_id: self.iface_id, net_bridge_chan_id: self.net_bridge_chan_id })
#         } else {
#             log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to get buffer pointer for TX DMA handle {}. Freeing it.", dma_handle));
#             if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(dma_handle) { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free TX DMA buffer (ptr error): {}", e)); } // Try to free if ptr couldn't be obtained
#             None
#         }
#     }
# }
#

"""## Update vnode/net-stack/main.rs

### Subtask:
Provide the complete and latest Rust code for vnode/net-stack/main.rs, ensuring it correctly initializes smoltcp and AetherNetDevice, handles IPC messages from net-bridge, and processes NetStackRequests from other V-Nodes.

**Reasoning**:
I need to provide the complete and latest Rust code for `vnode/net-stack/main.rs` as per the subtask instructions. This code will handle initialization of `smoltcp` and `AetherNetDevice`, manage IPC communication with both `net-bridge` and other V-Nodes, and process various `NetStackRequest`s including `SendTo`.
"""

# fn log(msg: &str) {
#     unsafe {
#         let res = syscall3(
#             SYS_LOG,
#             msg.as_ptr() as u64,
#             msg.len() as u64,
#             0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
#         );
#         if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
#     }
# }

# // Get current time from kernel
# fn get_current_time_ms() -> u64 {
#     // Assuming SYS_TIME returns ticks, convert to ms for smoltcp Instant
#     unsafe { syscall2(SYS_TIME, 0, 0) * 10 } // Assuming 1 tick = 10 ms for demo
# }

# #[no_mangle]
# pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
#     // Channel for requests from other V-Nodes (Socket API)
#     let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(3);
#     // Channel for data plane communication with net-bridge (RxPackets, TxPacketAcks)
#     let mut bridge_data_chan = VNodeChannel::new(2);
# 
#     log("AetherNet Service V-Node starting up...");
# 
#     // 1. Initialize AetherNetDevice to interact with the net-bridge driver
#     // Pass the channel ID for net-bridge communication
#     let mut device = AetherNetDevice::new(0, bridge_data_chan.id);
# 
#     // 2. Configure smoltcp interface
#     let ethernet_addr = EthernetAddress([0x02, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01]);
#     let config = Config::new(HardwareAddress::Ethernet(ethernet_addr));

# 
#     // Assign a static IP address
#     iface.update_ip_addrs(|addrs| {
#         addrs.push(IpCidr::new(IpAddress::v4(10, 0, 2, 15), 24)).unwrap();
#     });

# 
#     // 3. Initialize smoltcp SocketSet
#     let mut sockets_storage = Vec::new();
#     let mut sockets = SocketSet::new(sockets_storage);
# 
#     // 4. Socket Management
#     let mut next_socket_handle: u32 = 1;
#     let mut smoltcp_sockets_map: BTreeMap<u32, smoltcp::socket::SocketHandle> = BTreeMap::new(); // Maps our handle to smoltcp's
# 
#     // Main event loop for the network stack
#     loop {
#         let timestamp = Instant::from_millis(get_current_time_ms());
# 
#         // --- Handle Incoming Packets from net-bridge V-Node via IPC --- (from net-bridge to aethernet_device)
#         if let Ok(Some(net_msg_data)) = bridge_data_chan.recv_non_blocking() { // Check for messages from net-bridge
#             if let Ok(net_packet_msg) = postcard::from_bytes::<NetPacketMsg>(&net_msg_data) {
#                 match net_packet_msg {
#                     NetPacketMsg::RxPacket { dma_handle, len } => {
#                         log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Received RxPacket from net-bridge for handle: {}, len: {}", dma_handle, len));
#                         // Enqueue the received packet handle into the device for smoltcp to consume

#                         log("AetherNet: Received TxPacketAck from net-bridge.");
#                         // Handle TX acknowledgment if needed (e.g., update internal state)
#                     },
#                     _ => log("AetherNet: Received unexpected NetPacketMsg from net-bridge."),
#                 }
#             } else {
#                 log("AetherNet: Failed to deserialize NetPacketMsg from net-bridge.");
#             }
#         }
# 
#         // 1. Poll smoltcp interface for network events (e.g., ARP, ICMP, TCP/UDP activity)
#         // This call will trigger device.receive() and device.transmit() internally
#         iface.poll(timestamp, &mut device, &mut sockets);
#

#         if let Ok(Some(req_data)) = own_chan.recv_non_blocking() { // Check for messages from other V-Nodes
#             if let Ok(request) = postcard::from_bytes::<NetStackRequest>(&req_data) {
#                 log("AetherNet: Received request from another V-Node.");
#                 let response = match request {
#                     NetStackRequest::OpenSocket(sock_type, local_port) => {
#                         let handle = next_socket_handle;
#                         next_socket_handle += 1;
# 
#                         let smoltcp_socket = match sock_type {
#                             0 => { // TCP

#                                 socket
#                             },
#                             1 => { // UDP
#                                 log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Opening UDP socket on port {}", local_port));
#                                 let mut socket = UdpSocket::new(
#                                     smoltcp::socket::UdpSocketBuffer::new(Vec::new()),
#                                     smoltcp::socket::UdpSocketBuffer::new(Vec::new()),
#                                 );
#                                 if local_port != 0 { socket.bind(local_port).unwrap(); }
#                                 socket
#                             },
#                             _ => {
#                                 log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Invalid socket type {}", sock_type));
#                                 return NetStackResponse::Error(100); // Invalid socket type, cannot create socket
#                             }
#                         };
# 
#                         // Add socket to management
#                         sockets.add(smoltcp_socket);
#                         smoltcp_sockets_map.insert(handle, smoltcp::socket::SocketHandle::from(sockets.len() - 1)); // Correctly get smoltcp handle
#                         NetStackResponse::SocketOpened(handle)
#                     },
#                     NetStackRequest::Send(handle, data) => {
#                         log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Sending {} bytes on socket {}", data.len(), handle));

#                                     smoltcp::socket::Socket::Tcp(s) => {

#                                         NetStackResponse::Success
#                                     },
#                                     _ => NetStackResponse::Error(102), // Not a TCP/UDP socket
#                                 }
#                             } else { NetStackResponse::Error(103) } // Smoltcp Socket not found
#                         } else { NetStackResponse::Error(103) } // Our handle not found
#                     },
#                     NetStackRequest::SendTo(handle, remote_ip, remote_port, data) => {
#                         log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Sending {} bytes to {}:{} on UDP socket {}", data.len(), Ipv4Address::from_bytes(&remote_ip), remote_port, handle));
#                         if let Some(smoltcp_handle) = smoltcp_sockets_map.get(&handle) {
#                             if let Some(socket) = sockets.get_mut(*smoltcp_handle) {
#                                 match socket {

#                                         NetStackResponse::Success
#                                     },
#                                     _ => NetStackResponse::Error(102), // Not a UDP socket

#                     NetStackRequest::Recv(handle) => {
#                         log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Receiving on socket {}", handle));

#                              if let Some(socket) = sockets.get_mut(*smoltcp_handle) {

#                                         if let Ok((size, _endpoint)) = s.recv_slice(&mut buffer) {
#                                             buffer.resize(size, 0);

#                                         }
#                                          else {
#                                             NetStackResponse::Data(Vec::new())

#                                     },
#                                     _ => NetStackResponse::Error(102), // Not a TCP/UDP socket
